title: Send follow-up email to Alex after nucleus-phone walkthrough
description:
Pull the Fireflies transcript for the Alex / nucleus-phone walkthrough (search by participant name 'Alex' or by recent meeting date — this session ran OASIS burn, so the walkthrough was likely in the last 1-2 days).

**Steps:**
1. **Fetch transcript** — Use Fireflies GraphQL API per `memory/runbooks/fireflies-api.md`. Search by participant 'Alex' or recent date range. If rate-limited (50/day free tier), batch carefully.
2. **Extract:**
   - Commitments Tom made during the call
   - Commitments Alex made
   - Open questions / parking lot items
   - Any action items with owners
3. **Draft follow-up email** via `m365_create_draft` addressed to Alex (+19515286164 is his phone; email needs to be looked up — check HubSpot via joruva-gtm MCP if not in Fireflies metadata).
4. **Voice:** Tom's personal voice per `memory/runbooks/m365-email.md` (CHECK BEFORE drafting). Warm Authority, natural 'I', no hyphens (see `memory/feedback_no_hyphens.md`), no dead-giveaway LLM phrasing.
5. **Output:** Return the draft ID and a summary of the email body so Tom can review before sending.

**Context:**
- Alex = UCIL Vapi transfer destination (+19515286164)
- nucleus-phone = outbound sales dialer PWA, deployed https://nucleus-phone.onrender.com
- Parent project: UCIL
- Runbooks to consult: `fireflies-api.md`, `m365-email.md`, `graph-api.md`

**Why this is queued vs. done now:** The session that filed this bead didn't have mcp-m365 or Fireflies MCP tools loaded. Fresh session with full MCP access will execute cleanly.

title: Fix cleanCompanyName in resolve-signal-domains-v2.js to strip parentheses
description:
## Problem

`cleanCompanyName()` in `scripts/resolve-signal-domains-v2.js` doesn't strip parenthetical content. LinkedIn typeahead rejects queries containing parens with HTTP 400. ~2% error rate observed during the 2026-04-20 live resolution run (~50 companies affected of ~2,669).

Example failing names from journal:
- 'Proponent, Inc. dba (Proponent)'
- 'Boeing Company - Defense, Space and Security (BDS)'
- 'Meggitt (Erlanger), LLC'
- 'Prototek Digital Manufacturing (Madison)'

All journaled as `state: skipped, reason: linkedin_error` with `error: linkedin after 3 retries: linkedin 400`.

## Fix

In `cleanCompanyName()` add `n = n.replace(/\\s*\\([^)]*\\)/g, '').trim();` before the state-stripping step. Also consider stripping `dba` / `d/b/a` clauses entirely since they add noise to LinkedIn matching.

## Retry path

The journal captures these entries with structured reason. Build a one-shot `scripts/retry-linkedin-errors.js` that:
1. Reads journal for entries with `reason: linkedin_error`
2. Looks up the company_name from v35_lead_reservoir by old_domain
3. Re-runs `searchLinkedInTypeahead` with the fixed cleanCompanyName
4. On success, runs `searchGoogle` + `updateDomain`
5. Appends a new journal entry with the resolved state

Or simpler: add `--retry-errors` flag to resolve-signal-domains-v2.js that flips the resume filter to re-process `state: skipped, reason: linkedin_error` entries.

## Followup

After fix + retry, re-run `burn-spear-mobile.js` dry-run to measure hit-rate lift from recovered ~50 companies.

title: Disposition color mismatch: connected is blue in LastCallCard, green in Activity
description:
## Context

The web canonical for the `connected` disposition is inconsistent across two surfaces:

- `client/src/components/cockpit/LastCallCard.jsx:12` renders `connected` as **blue** (`var(--cockpit-blue-500)` for the bar, `--cockpit-blue-50` for pill bg, `--cockpit-blue-900` for pill text).
- `client/src/pages/Activity.jsx:21,34` renders `connected` as **green** (`bg-jv-green` for the disposition bar, `bg-jv-green/20 text-jv-green` for the pill).

Same disposition, two different visual treatments. Caused friction during native iOS `CallDisposition.swift` work in joruva-dialer-mac (joruva-dialer-mac-2b3) — Linus code review flagged that NucleusCore can only mirror one canonical at a time. The dialer currently follows `Activity.jsx` (green) since History is the closer analog, but the doc comment has to call out the inconsistency.

## Decision needed

Pick one:
1. `connected` is **blue** everywhere — info/neutral semantic ("connected, but not yet a hot lead").
2. `connected` is **green** everywhere — positive semantic ("the call connected, that's a win").

Then update whichever file is wrong.

## References

- iOS canonical map: `joruva-dialer-mac/Packages/NucleusCore/Sources/NucleusCore/CallDisposition.swift`
- Linus review notes: filed on bd `joruva-dialer-mac-2b3` round-2 review

title: Monitor 112 pending Apollo webhooks from burn-spear-mobile; escalate to support if >20% fail
description:
## Context
`scripts/burn-spear-mobile.js` fired 629 unique Apollo `/people/match` reveals (8+1 credits each) across 3 runs on 2026-04-20/21. As of burn completion, webhook delivery status:
- 515 contacts updated with `phone_type='mobile'` (81.9% yield)
- 112 contacts still at `phone=NULL` despite `apollo_person_id` set and reveal committed in journal
- 2 edge cases (non-mobile phone types stored by prior flows)

The 112 pending may resolve as late webhooks arrive (Apollo docs: 'up to several minutes') or may be silent delivery failures — the exact failure mode that justified the original 5,592 credit refund.

## Action
1. Query `v35_pb_contacts` with `enrichment_batch_id='burn-spear-mobile' AND phone IS NULL` on 2026-04-22 (24hr after burn).
2. If count is still ~112 (no late deliveries), those reveals represent webhook-drop failures.
3. Escalate to Apollo support (ticket path: tech support, Francis/Melanie/Israel channel per memory/runbooks/apollo-api.md line 48-50). 112 failed × 9 credits = ~1,008 credits eligible for refund dispute. Precedent exists (5,592 refund on 2026-04-16).

## Related
- `memory/runbooks/apollo-api.md` — Apollo webhook delivery failure pattern + refund escalation path
- Journal: `~/.joruva/apollo-burn-journal.jsonl` has full audit trail of every attempt/commit

title: Add raw payload logging to Apollo webhook handler
description:
## Context

Before building any apollo_person_id infrastructure, we need to confirm Apollo is actually sending webhook callbacks and what they contain. The current webhook handler at `/api/apollo/phone-webhook` processes payloads but has no raw logging — failed matches are completely silent.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 0b

## What to change

### File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/routes/apollo-webhook.js` (line 18-20)

Add raw payload logging at the very top of the handler, before any parsing:

```js
console.log('Apollo webhook raw:', JSON.stringify(req.body).substring(0, 500));
```

Also add failure logging: when both email match and name fallback fail (updated === 0), log the full payload as a warning instead of silently returning 200 OK.

### Current behavior (lines 52-81)

The handler tries email match, then name fallback, but if both fail it just returns `{ received: true, updated: 0 }` with zero logging. This is why 1,065 contacts are missing phones — we have no visibility into webhook delivery failures.

## After deploy

Trigger one test reveal with `reveal_phone_number: true` and check Render logs for:
- Did a webhook arrive at all?
- Does the payload contain `person.id` (apollo_person_id)?
- Does the payload contain `person.phone_numbers`?
- Does `person.email` in the webhook match what we stored?

Results determine the shape of the Phase 2 issue (nucleus-phone webhook matching fix).

## Acceptance criteria

- Raw payload logged (truncated to 500 chars) on every webhook hit
- Failed matches (updated === 0) logged as warnings with payload details
- Deployed to Render so we can observe real webhook traffic
close_reason: Deployed: raw payload logging + unmatched webhook warning in apollo-webhook.js

title: Follow-up: web playback — swap <a href={recording_url}> for signed-URL HTML5 audio player
description:
## Context

Filed during M1 dialer planning (`/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md` follow-up #10). Not blocking M1 (iOS) — separate web-only fix.

Today the web client renders `<a href={recording_url}>` where `recording_url` is a raw `api.twilio.com` URL requiring Twilio account-SID basic auth. Clicking it opens a Twilio basic-auth dialog. **There is no working playback on web today.**

## What to do

After M1 ships the signed-URL endpoint (`nucleus-phone-op7` — `GET /api/recording/:callId/signed-url`), update the web client:

1. Replace `<a href={recording_url}>` (find call sites — search `recording_url` in `client/src/`)
2. On render of a call detail with a recording (`recording_duration != null`), call `GET /api/recording/:callId/signed-url`
3. Render `<audio controls src={signedUrl} />` instead of the raw link
4. Re-fetch the signed URL if the user keeps the page open longer than 5 min (TTL)

## Acceptance

- Click "play recording" in web call detail view → audio plays inline, no Twilio dialog
- Tested in Chrome + Safari

## Blocks

Nothing M1-critical. Caller team currently uses iOS app for recordings; web is fallback.

## Depends on

- `nucleus-phone-op7` (signed-URL + stream proxy live)

## References

Plan file: `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md` (follow-ups section)
Parent epic of M1 work: `joruva-dialer-mac-vq4`

title: Backfill oid for existing nucleus_phone_users via Graph batch lookup
description:
## Context

`nucleus-phone-t3x` added an `oid` column to `nucleus_phone_users` and stamps it on first authenticated /api/auth/exchange call. This means existing users only get their `oid` set when they next log in via the native iOS dialer.

For the 7+ seeded internal users (tom, paul, kate, britt, ryann, alex, lily), this is fine — they'll log in soon enough. But if we ever want to use `oid` as the canonical join key (instead of email), every active user needs the column populated.

## What to implement

A one-shot script (`scripts/backfill-user-oids.ts` or similar) that:
1. Queries `nucleus_phone_users WHERE oid IS NULL AND is_active = TRUE`
2. For each row, calls Microsoft Graph `GET /users/{email}` to retrieve `id` (the oid)
3. UPDATE `nucleus_phone_users SET oid =  WHERE id = `
4. Handles 404 (user not in tenant — skip + log) and the same UNIQUE collision error path the route handles

Use the joruva-mcp-m365 client_credentials auth pattern — script doesn't need a user session.

## Acceptance
- Script runs cleanly against staging
- All active users have `oid` populated
- Idempotent (re-running is a no-op)

## References
- Plan file: `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md` — section 'Plan-review fixes folded in', item 11
- Sibling bead: nucleus-phone-t3x (the route that stamps oid on first login)

title: Scope phone reveals to SPEAR + TARGETED tiers only
description:
## Context

Phone reveals cost 8 Apollo credits each. AWARENESS tier companies should get email-only reveals (1 credit). This is the single biggest lever for reducing credit burn — AWARENESS is the largest tier by volume.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 3

## What to change

### 1. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/apollo.js`

In `searchPeopleByCompany()` (line 121), add a `requestPhone` parameter (default: true for backward compat):

```js
async function searchPeopleByCompany(domain, titleFilters = DEFAULT_TITLE_FILTERS, perPage = 10, requestPhone = true)
```

When `requestPhone=false`: call `revealPerson(preview.id, false)` — 1 credit, no phone webhook.
When `requestPhone=true`: call `revealPerson(preview.id, true)` — 8 credits, async phone delivery.

Credit counting must reflect this: `creditsUsed += requestPhone ? PHONE_REVEAL_CREDIT_COST : 1`

### 2. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/signal-enrichment.js`

In `runBatchEnrichment()`, determine `requestPhone` based on company's signal tier before calling `searchPeopleByCompany()`:
- SPEAR, TARGETED → `requestPhone: true`
- AWARENESS → `requestPhone: false`

The tier is available on the company row from the signal query.

### 3. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/scripts/run-enrichment-uncapped.js`

Same tier-based `requestPhone` logic. The script already queries companies with their signal_tier.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: nucleus-phone-2xv (credit accounting fix — needs PHONE_REVEAL_CREDIT_COST constant and correct credit counting)

## Acceptance criteria

- `searchPeopleByCompany()` accepts `requestPhone` parameter
- AWARENESS companies enriched with `requestPhone=false` (1 credit per reveal)
- SPEAR/TARGETED companies enriched with `requestPhone=true` (8 credits per reveal)
- Both signal-enrichment.js and run-enrichment-uncapped.js use tier-based logic
- Verify: run enrichment for one AWARENESS company, confirm `reveal_phone_number` is NOT sent
close_reason: Completed: requestPhone option added to searchPeopleByCompany, awareness locked out of VALID_TIERS

title: Vapi POST call/{id}/stop returns 404 - end-call doesn't actually stop the call
description:
## Discovered via debug system 2026-04-14

The new debug logging surfaced a real Vapi API bug on the first practice call after deploy:

```
POST call/019d8a63-eb3c-7eed-951a-e93883cc140f/stop failed: 404
Body: {"message":"Cannot POST /call/019d8a63-eb3c-7eed-951a-e93883cc140f/stop","error":"Not Found","statusCode":404}
```

The endpoint fired twice from the same call (likely a retry).

## Impact

When a user clicks 'End Call' in the cockpit, the client tells the server to stop the Vapi call. The server hits Vapi's stop endpoint, which 404s. Result: the Vapi call doesn't actually terminate from our side — it only ends when Vapi's own inactivity timeout kicks in. This may explain why some calls feel like they 'hang' after End Call is pressed.

## Investigation needed

1. Check current Vapi API docs for the correct call-stop endpoint (path may have changed from `/call/{id}/stop` to something else, or moved under `/call/{id}`)
2. Look at `server/lib/vapi.js` for the stopCall function
3. Test the corrected path
4. Update and verify via debug events: a successful stop should generate a `vapi.api` integration event with no error level

## Reference

- Discovered via `/api/debug/events` after a practice call
- Plan: ~/.claude/plans/synthetic-wondering-mountain.md (production debug mode)
- Bead this followed: nucleus-phone-9el (deploy DEBUG=1 + verification)
close_reason: Fixed in da0094a (DELETE /call/{id}). Follow-up hardening in 60b480b, 0735c7a, ff6561b covered structured errors, honest error handling, stopCallAndLog helper, 18 tests. Remaining cancel-route silent-success gap tracked in nucleus-phone-nja.

title: Fix Apollo credit accounting: 8 credits per phone reveal + stop clobbering budget_limit
description:
## Context

Every revealPerson() call with reveal_phone_number=true costs 8 mobile credits, but the ledger counts 1. This 8x undercount drained a 1,000 credit/month Basic plan in 8 days. The APOLLO_DAILY_BUDGET constant (400) must drop to 50 (approved). Additionally, nucleus-phone's incrementApolloBudget() clobbers the budget_limit column that V3.5 owns.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 1

## What to change

### 1. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/apollo.js`

In `searchPeopleByCompany()` (line ~172-179), the credit counter does `creditsUsed++` per reveal. Fix:

- Add constant at top of file: `const PHONE_REVEAL_CREDIT_COST = 8;`
- Change `creditsUsed++` (line 179) to `creditsUsed += PHONE_REVEAL_CREDIT_COST`
- Also return `apollo_person_id` from `revealPerson()` (line 97-105) — add `apollo_person_id: p.id` to the return object. This is needed by Phase 2 but is a trivial addition here.

### 2. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/signal-enrichment.js`

**Line 18**: Change `APOLLO_DAILY_BUDGET = 400` to `APOLLO_DAILY_BUDGET = 50` (approved by Tom)

**Lines 50-64** (`incrementApolloBudget`): The UPSERT passes `APOLLO_DAILY_BUDGET` as `budget_limit`, overwriting whatever V3.5 set. Fix: remove `budget_limit` from the INSERT — let V3.5 own that column. Nucleus-phone should only increment `consumed`.

Changed query should be:
```sql
INSERT INTO v35_credit_daily_ledger (ledger_date, service, consumed, last_increment_at)
VALUES (CURRENT_DATE, 'apollo', $1, NOW())
ON CONFLICT (ledger_date, service) DO UPDATE SET
  consumed = v35_credit_daily_ledger.consumed + $1,
  last_increment_at = NOW()
RETURNING consumed, remaining, pct_consumed
```
(Single parameter instead of two)

### 3. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/scripts/run-enrichment-uncapped.js`

This script accumulates `totalCredits` locally but never calls `incrementApolloBudget()`. The shared ledger doesn't know about credits this script burns.

Fix: After each company enrichment, call `incrementApolloBudget(result.creditsUsed)`. Import from signal-enrichment.js or duplicate the function.

Also review `CREDIT_CAP` (line 36, currently 5000). With 8x credit cost, this now caps at ~625 actual reveals. Document the new effective limit in a comment.

## Acceptance criteria

- `PHONE_REVEAL_CREDIT_COST = 8` constant in apollo.js
- `creditsUsed` increments by 8 per phone reveal, not 1
- `APOLLO_DAILY_BUDGET = 50` in signal-enrichment.js
- `incrementApolloBudget()` no longer clobbers `budget_limit`
- `run-enrichment-uncapped.js` wired to shared ledger
- `revealPerson()` returns `apollo_person_id` in its result object
close_reason: Deployed: PHONE_REVEAL_CREDIT_COST=8, APOLLO_DAILY_BUDGET=50, run-enrichment-uncapped wired to ledger

title: Seed equipment data (80-120 entries)
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/giggly-rolling-canyon.md (Phase 6)
Codebase: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/

Seed the equipment_catalog and equipment_details tables with 80-120 entries covering CNC, packaging, collision/paint, and woodworking equipment. This can run as soon as Phase 1 (DB schema) is complete — independent of the real-time pipeline.

## What to implement

### `scripts/seed-equipment-specs.js` (NEW)

Run manually: `node scripts/seed-equipment-specs.js`

80-120 entries covering:
- **CNC (40+):** Haas VF-1 through VF-6, ST-10/20/30/35, Mini Mill, UMC-500/750. Mazak QTN-100/200/250/300, VTC-300C, INTEGREX. DMG Mori NLX 2500, CMX 600V. Okuma GENOS L/M series, LB3000. Doosan DNM 4500/5700, Lynx 2100. Fanuc ROBODRILL. Hurco VM10/20/30, VMX. Kitamura Mycenter.
- **Packaging (10+):** Pearson CE25/CE35/CE50. Wexxar WF20. Loveshaw LDX.
- **Collision/Paint (15+):** SATA/DeVilbiss/Iwata HVLP guns by orifice size. Dynabrade/3M DA sanders. Clemco/Schmidt sandblasters.
- **Woodworking (10+):** Shopbot PRSalpha/Buddy. Laguna SmartShop. Thermwood CNC routers.

Each entry includes: manufacturer, model, model_variants (STT garbling variants like 'VF2'/'VF 2'), category, subcategory, cfm_min, cfm_max, cfm_typical, psi_required, duty_cycle_pct, air_quality_class, source ('manufacturer' or 'expert_input'), confidence ('medium'), verified_by ('system'), and matching equipment_details record where applicable.

Uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (manufacturer, model) DO UPDATE` for idempotency.

Script uses `pool` from `server/db.js`. Connects, runs inserts in a transaction, reports count, disconnects.

**Important decisions from plan:**
- Ships without Alex review. Alex reviews post-launch.
- `confidence: 'medium'`, `verified_by: 'system'` for all seed data.
- Dryer/filter entries have `price: null`. Widget shows "pricing available on request."

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script runs without errors: `node scripts/seed-equipment-specs.js`
- [ ] Idempotent: running twice doesn't create duplicates
- [ ] `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM equipment_catalog` returns 80+
- [ ] `SELECT * FROM equipment_catalog WHERE manufacturer = 'Haas' AND model = 'VF-2'` returns correct CFM values
- [ ] model_variants populated (e.g., Haas VF-2 has variants like 'VF2', 'VF 2')
- [ ] equipment_details records exist for entries with known details
- [ ] All 4 categories represented: cnc_mill, cnc_lathe, packaging, paint, woodworking

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nucleus-phone-axr (equipment_catalog + equipment_details tables must exist)
close_reason: Completed

title: Add hover-over acronym glossary to Compressed Air Fundamentals guide
description:
## Context

Blake Zweigle (commission rep, started 2026-04-20) raised this on his 2026-04-28 onboarding call with Tom. Recorded in Fireflies transcript `01KQ9A85A7YSYXNP6H0PFVE186` at 16:17.

**Blake's exact ask:** "have a living acronym glossary dictionary. So if you hover over an acronym, it just pops up and references like shows you what that acronym [means]"

He suggested a dotted underline UI pattern (like iOS lookup), and even floated a "Wi-Fi signal bar that shows level of importance" indicator on each definition. The first part (hover dictionary) is the high-value piece.

**Tom committed to delivering this** in his email to Blake (2026-04-28, blake@joruva.com): "Acronym hover glossary in the Fundamentals guide. Filed. It'll land soon."

## Where it goes

- Source: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/public/compressed-air-fundamentals.html` (Paul's revised v2)
- Same page is served at https://nucleus-phone.onrender.com/compressed-air-fundamentals

## Suggested implementation

1. Add a `<dfn>` or `<abbr>` tag wrapping every acronym occurrence (CFM, PSI, VSD, VFD, NPT, ACFM, SCFM, etc.)
2. Style `abbr[title]` with `text-decoration: underline dotted`; native browser tooltip on hover already works for desktop.
3. For mobile (no hover), add a `title` attribute + small JS that shows the definition on tap below the term inline.
4. Keep the glossary data inline in the page (no external dependency) so it works offline once the PWA is installed.

## Acronyms to cover (initial list)

CFM, ACFM, SCFM, PSI, PSIA, PSIG, HP, kW, VSD, VFD, PM (motor), NPT, BSP, CAGI, AS9100, ISO 9001, OSHA, EPA, kWh.

title: Set up Jest + supertest framework and test helpers
description:
**Install test framework and create shared mocking infrastructure.**

**Steps:**
1. `npm install --save-dev jest supertest`
2. Create `jest.config.js` at repo root:
   - `testEnvironment: 'node'`
   - `roots: ['server']`
   - `forceExit: true` (conference.js has module-level `setInterval` without `.unref()`)
   - `clearMocks: true`
3. Add scripts to `package.json`: `"test": "jest"`, `"test:watch": "jest --watch"`
4. Create `server/__tests__/helpers/mock-pool.js` -- factory returning `{ pool: { query: jest.fn(), connect: jest.fn() } }`
5. Create `server/__tests__/helpers/mock-fetch.js` -- `mockFetchResponse()` factory with setup/teardown helpers

**Key gotchas from the plan:**
- conference.js `setInterval` fires on `require()` -- `forceExit: true` prevents Jest hanging
- claude.js uses `.unref()` so no special handling needed
- team.json mocks must include `identity` + `name` fields for nameMap construction

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `npm test` runs and exits cleanly (0 tests initially, that's fine)
- Both helper files export working factory functions
- jest.config.js has all required settings

**Plan:** `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/deep-fluttering-wren.md` (Sections 1-2)
close_reason: Jest + supertest framework with mock helpers in place

title: Follow-up: backfill oid for existing nucleus_phone_users via Microsoft Graph batch lookup
description:
## Context

Filed during M1 dialer planning (`/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md` follow-up #11). Not blocking M1.

Migration `006_users_oid.sql` (filed in `nucleus-phone-t3x`) adds the `oid` column. The exchange route writes `oid` only on next-login per user. Existing users won't have `oid` populated until they personally sign in via the iOS dialer.

For M1 this is fine (lookups remain email-based per Architecture decision #5). But long-term, oid is the canonical join key (mailbox renames break email-based identity), and an empty `oid` column is an embarrassing data-quality issue.

## What to do

1. Write a one-off script `server/scripts/backfill-oid.js`:
   - Read all `nucleus_phone_users` where `oid IS NULL` and `is_active = true`
   - Use Microsoft Graph batch endpoint (`POST /v1.0/$batch`) to look up each by `userPrincipalName` (email)
   - Reuse the m365 client_credentials flow already present in `joruva-mcp-m365` if shareable, otherwise inline a fresh service-principal token grab against `https://graph.microsoft.com/.default`
   - Write `oid` back to DB; log successes + failures
   - Idempotent (only touches NULL oid rows)
2. Run once against staging, verify, then prod.
3. Optionally schedule as a monthly Render cron after one clean run.

## Acceptance

- All active `nucleus_phone_users` rows have `oid` populated post-run
- No errors in run log (or all errors documented for manual cleanup)

## Depends on

- `nucleus-phone-t3x` (migration must exist; column must be present)

## References

Plan file: `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md` (Architecture decision #5 + follow-up #11)

title: Fix backfill script + build phone reconciliation script
description:
## Context

backfill-apollo-phones.js has a misleading "0 credits" comment — it actually calls reveal with reveal_phone_number=true (8 credits each). Need to fix the default behavior and build a new reconciliation script that detects contacts stuck with phone=NULL.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 4

## What to change

### 1. File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/scripts/backfill-apollo-phones.js`

Current problem (lines 33-39): `matchByEmail()` sends `reveal_phone_number: true` — this costs 8 credits per call, NOT 0 as the comment on line 10 claims.

Fix:
- Default: `reveal_phone_number: false` (1 credit, re-fetch cached data — may NOT include phone)
- Add `--with-phone` CLI flag for explicit phone reveal (8 credits)
- Fix the "Re-matching by email costs 0 credits" comment (line 10) to accurately state the cost
- Review `--limit` default (currently 600, line 31) — may need adjustment

### 2. New file: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/scripts/reconcile-apollo-phones.js`

Find contacts that should have received phone webhooks but didn't:

```sql
SELECT id, full_name, email, apollo_person_id, domain
FROM v35_pb_contacts
WHERE source = 'apollo' AND phone IS NULL AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
ORDER BY created_at DESC
```

For each:
- Re-match by email with `reveal_phone_number: false` (1 credit) — check if sync response includes phone
- If phone found in sync response, update the contact
- Report: X reconciled, Y still pending

CLI flags:
- `--dry-run` — report only, don't update
- `--limit N` — cap number of contacts to process (default 100)
- `--with-phone` — use reveal_phone_number=true (8 credits) for stubborn cases

Wire to n8n cron (every 6 hours) after initial manual validation.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: nucleus-phone-vtk (webhook matching fix — needs apollo_person_id column to exist)

## Acceptance criteria

- backfill script defaults to `reveal_phone_number: false` (1 credit)
- backfill script has `--with-phone` flag for 8-credit reveals
- Misleading "0 credits" comment fixed
- New reconciliation script finds phone=NULL contacts older than 1 hour
- Reconciliation script has `--dry-run`, `--limit`, `--with-phone` flags
- Reconciliation script reports results clearly (X reconciled, Y pending)
close_reason: Completed: backfill script rewritten with --with-phone flag, webhook_url inclusion, apollo_person_id backfill mode. Reconciliation is now the backfill script itself (no separate script needed).

title: Refactor server/index.js to export app for testing
description:
**Prerequisite for all test work.** Currently `start()` runs immediately on require, which means supertest can't import the app without side effects (DB connect, port bind).

**Changes needed in `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/index.js`:**
1. Add `module.exports = { app }` to export the Express app
2. Guard `start()` with `if (require.main === module)` so it only runs when executed directly
3. Verify the server still starts normally via `npm start`

**Why:** supertest needs to import the app object without triggering DB connections or port binding. Without this, route-level tests (scoreboard.test.js) are impossible.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `node server/index.js` still starts the server normally
- `require('./server/index')` returns `{ app }` without side effects
- No production behavior changes

**Plan:** `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/deep-fluttering-wren.md` (Section 0)
close_reason: Already implemented — index.js exports app with require.main guard

description:
## Context

Cancel QuickBooks Online, saving $1,490/year. All accounting functions handled by Mercury API + Nucleus.

title: Multichannel ABM API still returns old field names (intent_tier/intent_score instead of signal_tier/signal_score)
description:
## Context

The plan (Phase 2, kc8.2) specified that `/admin/abm/accounts` in joruva-multichannel should join `v35_signal_metadata` to return signal fields (`signal_tier`, `signal_score`, `cert_expiry_date`, `contract_total`, `source_count`, `dod_flag`).

## Current behavior

`GET /admin/abm/accounts` returns the old field names:
- `intent_tier` instead of `signal_tier`
- `intent_score` instead of `signal_score`
- `contact_count` / `personas_covered` (no signal metadata joined)

## Impact

- Pipeline tab proxy (`GET /api/signals/pipeline`) passes through MC response, so it also lacks signal fields
- Signal Contacts routes (checks 2-3) work fine because they query V3.5 directly
- Cockpit (check 7) works fine because it queries `v35_signal_metadata` directly

## Fix

In `/Users/Shared/joruva-multichannel/src/lib/account-manager.ts`, `listAccounts()` needs to batch-fetch from `v35_signal_metadata` and merge signal fields into the response (as described in plan Phase 2).

## Verification

Run: `node scripts/verify-phone-integration.js` — checks 1 and 8 should pass after fix.

## Plan ref
`~/.claude/plans/toasty-knitting-marshmallow.md` — Phase 2
close_reason: Fixed in joruva-multichannel ef2d1bb. listAccounts now defaults signal fields to null when no v35_signal_metadata exists. All 36 integration checks pass.

title: Investigate equipment double-counting in practice sim sizing
description:
## Context

Kate reported that the Reactor recommends equipment that's too large 2/3 times during Mike Garza practice calls. The 3rd time it was too small — suggesting the same unit that was 'too large' before.

## Root Cause Hypothesis

When Mike mentions equipment in stages — first 'I've got 5 CNC machines', then 'three Haas VF-2s and two Mazak QTN-200s' — the entity extractor likely creates SEPARATE entries for each mention without deduplicating:

1. CNC x5 → 8 CFM × 0.75 × 5 = 30 CFM (from generic 'CNC' mention)
2. Haas VF-2 x3 → 8 × 0.75 × 3 = 18 CFM (from specific model mention)
3. Mazak QTN-200 x2 → 5 × 0.70 × 2 = 7 CFM (from specific model mention)
4. Total: 55 CFM → recommends JRS-15E instead of correct JRS-10E (42 CFM)

The equipment-curator (`addVariant` in `equipment-curator.js`) should handle deduplication but may not catch generic→specific overlap ('CNC' vs 'Haas VF-2').

## Investigation Plan

1. Add logging to `equipment-pipeline.js` to dump equipment list before sizing
2. Run a practice call on Easy and capture the equipment entries
3. Check `equipment-curator.js` deduplication logic for generic→specific overlap
4. Fix dedup or adjust `CATEGORY_DEFAULTS` if CFM estimates are off

## Files

- `server/lib/equipment-pipeline.js` — orchestrates extraction → lookup → sizing
- `server/lib/entity-extractor.js` — Claude-based equipment extraction from transcript
- `server/lib/equipment-curator.js` — deduplication via `addVariant`
- `server/lib/sizing-engine.js` — demand calculation + system recommendation
- `server/config/sim-contacts/mike-garza-easy.json` — expected: JRS-10E (42 CFM)
close_reason: Fixed: category supersession + proximity window + verbal reset. 50 tests. Deployed.

title: Scoreboard API + client page
description:
## Phase D, Bead 10 — Scoreboard API + Page

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 365-397)

### What
Build the team scoreboard: API endpoint for rolling 7-day stats, nightly aggregation endpoint, client page with leaderboard, and Slack milestone alerts.

### Create: `server/routes/scoreboard.js`

Replace the stub file created in Bead 6.

**Endpoints:**

`GET /api/scoreboard` (apiKeyAuth):
- Rolling 7-day stats from `nucleus_phone_calls`:
  ```sql
  SELECT caller_identity, COUNT(*) AS calls_made,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE qualification IN ('hot','warm')) AS leads_qualified,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE qualification = 'hot') AS hot_leads,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE disposition = 'callback_requested') AS callbacks,
    AVG(duration_seconds)::int AS avg_duration
  FROM nucleus_phone_calls
  WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' AND status = 'completed'
  GROUP BY caller_identity ORDER BY leads_qualified DESC, calls_made DESC
  ```
- **NOTE:** `qualification` values are VARCHAR `'hot'`/`'warm'` in `nucleus_phone_calls`, NOT `'qualified_hot'`/`'qualified_warm'` as in `customer_interactions.disposition`
- Map `caller_identity` short names ("tom", "kate") to display names using `server/config/team.json`

`POST /api/scoreboard/aggregate` (apiKeyAuth):
- Nightly n8n cron to materialize `ucil_agent_stats`
- Rolls up `nucleus_phone_calls` data into the stats table

### Create Client Files

**`client/src/pages/Scoreboard.jsx`:**
- Leaderboard cards per agent
- Sparklines for daily trends
- Streak indicators

**`client/src/hooks/useScoreboard.js`:**
- Fetch hook for scoreboard data

### Modify Existing Files

**`client/src/lib/api.js`:**
- Add `getScoreboard()`

**`client/src/App.jsx`:**
- Add `/scoreboard` route

**`client/src/components/layout/Shell.jsx`:**
- Add Score tab: `{ path: '/scoreboard', label: 'Score', icon: '🏆' }`
- Currently at line ~15 in the navigation items array

### Slack Celebrations (extend `server/lib/slack.js`)
- First qualified lead -> `formatMilestoneAlert()`
- Personal best calls in a day -> `formatMilestoneAlert()`
- 3+ day streak -> `formatStreakAlert()`

### Dependencies
- Requires `ucil_agent_stats` table (Bead 2: nucleus-phone-5j1.2)
- Requires `server/config/team.json` (created in Bead 8, but can be created here too if Bead 8 hasn't run -- just create the file)
- Route mount in index.js already done in Bead 6 (stub replacement)

### Testing
- GET /api/scoreboard with test data -> correct aggregation
- Verify `caller_identity` mapped to display names
- POST /api/scoreboard/aggregate -> materializes stats correctly
- Slack milestone fires on first qualified lead
- Client renders leaderboard cards

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `server/routes/scoreboard.js` replaces stub with full implementation
- [ ] GET endpoint returns rolling 7-day stats with display names
- [ ] POST endpoint materializes `ucil_agent_stats`
- [ ] `client/src/pages/Scoreboard.jsx` with leaderboard UI
- [ ] Score tab added to Shell navigation
- [ ] Route added to App.jsx
- [ ] Slack milestone alerts in `server/lib/slack.js`
close_reason: Committed in 1293335

title: n8n cron workflows for Fireflies sync + scoreboard aggregation
description:
## Phase E, Bead 12 — n8n Workflows

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 410-416)

### What
Create two n8n workflows to automate the Fireflies sync and scoreboard aggregation via cron triggers.

### Workflow 1: Fireflies Sync
- **Trigger:** Cron every 30 minutes
- **Action:** `POST https://nucleus-phone.onrender.com/api/fireflies-sync`
- **Headers:** `x-api-key: <NUCLEUS_PHONE_API_KEY>` (key value: `7HiiWgouyuepJPODV38YeDbTNVQi34Iv`)
- **Error handling:** Alert on failure (Slack notification)

### Workflow 2: Scoreboard Aggregation
- **Trigger:** Cron daily at 2am MST (9:00 UTC)
- **Action:** `POST https://nucleus-phone.onrender.com/api/scoreboard/aggregate`
- **Headers:** `x-api-key: <NUCLEUS_PHONE_API_KEY>`
- **Error handling:** Alert on failure

### Implementation
Use `n8n_create_workflow` MCP tool to create both workflows programmatically.

### Dependencies
- Requires Fireflies sync endpoint deployed (Bead 8: nucleus-phone-5j1.8)
- Requires Scoreboard aggregate endpoint deployed (Bead 10: nucleus-phone-5j1.10)
- Both endpoints must be live on Render before workflows are activated

### Testing
- Manually trigger each workflow via n8n UI
- Verify Fireflies sync workflow hits the endpoint and returns success
- Verify scoreboard aggregation materializes stats

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fireflies sync workflow created and active (30-min cron)
- [ ] Scoreboard aggregation workflow created and active (daily 2am MST)
- [ ] Both workflows use correct API key header
- [ ] Error alerting configured

title: Database schema migration for cockpit tables
description:
## Phase A, Bead 2 — Database Schema

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 110-115)

### What
Extend `initSchema()` in `server/db.js` to create two new tables needed by the scoreboard and sync systems.

### Modify: `server/db.js`

Add to `initSchema()`:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ucil_agent_stats (
  agent_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  stat_date DATE NOT NULL,
  calls_made INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  callbacks_done INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  leads_qualified INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  hot_leads INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  avg_call_duration INTEGER,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  PRIMARY KEY (agent_name, stat_date)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ucil_sync_state (
  sync_key VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
  last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  last_record_id TEXT,
  metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

Also add index: `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_agent_stats_agent_date ON ucil_agent_stats(agent_name, stat_date DESC);`

### Context
- `server/db.js` contains `initSchema()` which runs on server start
- `ucil_agent_stats` is used by the scoreboard (Bead 10) for materialized nightly stats
- `ucil_sync_state` is used by Fireflies sync (Bead 8) for checkpoint tracking and by Apollo/Dropcontact credit budgets (Bead 9)

### Testing
- Server starts without errors
- `\dt ucil_*` shows both tables in Postgres
- Tables have correct columns and constraints

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Server starts and both tables are created
- [ ] `ucil_agent_stats` has composite PK on (agent_name, stat_date)
- [ ] `ucil_sync_state` has PK on sync_key
- [ ] Index on ucil_agent_stats(agent_name, stat_date DESC) exists

title: Claude rapport intelligence module
description:
## Phase B, Bead 5 — Claude Rapport Module

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 184-197)

### What
Create the Claude API integration module that generates pre-call rapport intelligence from assembled contact data.

### Create: `server/lib/claude.js` (~180 lines)

**Functions:**
- `generateRapportIntel(contactData)` — calls Claude `/v1/messages` endpoint with structured prompt
  - Input: assembled contact data (identity, interactions, QA results, discovery data, PB contact data, company data)
  - Output: `{ rapport_starters, intel_nuggets, opening_line, adapted_script, watch_outs, product_reference }`
  - Model: `claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514`
  - Raw `fetch` to `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` (no SDK dependency)
- `clearCache(key)` — for "Refresh Intel" button

**Caching:**
- 15-min in-memory Map cache
- Cache key: prefer `hubspotContactId`, fall back to normalized phone
- Avoids duplicate cache entries when same contact resolves by different identifiers

**Timeout / Fallback:**
- **6-second AbortController timeout** on fetch — pre-call screen cannot block
- On Claude error/timeout: return `{ fallback: true, opening_line: "Hi [name]...", rapport_starters: [extracted from raw data] }`

**System Prompt:** Rapport-first intelligence analyst for Joruva Industrial. Produce JSON with: rapport_starters (from career history, headline, company vernacular, location, tenure -- NEVER fabricate), intel_nuggets (buying signals, objection prep, compliance), opening_line, adapted_script, watch_outs, product_reference.

**Env var:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (must be added to Render env, same key as joruva-ucil service)

### Testing
- Mock Claude response -> verify structured output parsing
- Mock Claude 500 -> verify fallback object returned
- Mock Claude timeout (>6s) -> verify AbortController fires, fallback returned
- Cache test: 2 calls within 15 min with same key -> Claude API called only once
- `clearCache(key)` -> next call hits Claude API again

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `server/lib/claude.js` exists with `generateRapportIntel` and `clearCache` exports
- [ ] Uses raw `fetch` (no SDK)
- [ ] 6-second AbortController timeout
- [ ] Fallback response on any error/timeout
- [ ] 15-min in-memory cache with canonical key logic
- [ ] `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var used (not hardcoded)

title: Identity resolution waterfall
description:
## Phase A, Bead 3 — Identity Resolution Waterfall

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 118-148)

### What
Create the identity resolver that unifies contact data from HubSpot and PB contacts into a single `ResolvedIdentity` object. Steps 1-2 only (HubSpot + PB). Steps 3-4 (Apollo/Dropcontact) added later in Bead 9.

### Create: `server/lib/identity-resolver.js` (~150 lines)

**Function:** `resolve(identifier) -> ResolvedIdentity`

**Steps:**
1. Parse identifier type (phone / email / hubspot-id)
2. **HubSpot lookup:** `findContactByPhone(phone)` or `getContact(id)` or `searchContacts(email)` (all from `server/lib/hubspot.js` extended in Bead 1)
   - Yields: name, email, company, hubspotContactId, hubspotCompanyId
3. **PB Contacts lookup:** Query `v35_pb_contacts` by `company_name_norm = normalized(company)` first, then filter by name similarity (first_name + last_name ILIKE)
   - **CRITICAL matching rules:**
     - If multiple matches, prefer: exact name match > first+last match > first-name-only match
     - If company match returns >5 rows and no name match, skip (avoid false positives)
     - **NEVER return PB data without both company AND name matching** -- wrong person's LinkedIn photo is worse than no photo
   - Yields: title, linkedinUrl, profileImage, summary, durationInRole, pastExperience, connectionDegree
4. Return unified `ResolvedIdentity` object

**ResolvedIdentity shape:**
```javascript
{
  resolved: boolean,
  hubspotContactId, hubspotCompanyId,
  name, email, phone, company, title,
  linkedinUrl, profileImage,
  pbContactData: { summary, durationInRole, pastExperience, connectionDegree },
  source: 'hubspot' | 'pb_contacts' | 'unknown'
}
```

### Dependencies
- Requires `server/lib/hubspot.js` with `findContactByPhone`, `getContact`, `searchContacts` (Bead 1: nucleus-phone-5j1.1)
- Requires `server/lib/phone.js` for `normalizePhone` (Bead 1)
- Requires `server/lib/company-normalizer.js` for `normalizeCompanyName` (Bead 1)
- Uses `server/db.js` pool for `v35_pb_contacts` queries

### PB Contacts Table Reference
- Table: `v35_pb_contacts`
- Key columns: `company_name_norm`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `linkedin_url`, `profile_image_url`, `headline`, `raw_data` (JSONB with summary, duration, past experience, connection degree)

### Testing
- Mock HubSpot -> returns contact -> verify `source: 'hubspot'`
- Mock HubSpot -> null, PB query -> rows with matching name -> verify `source: 'pb_contacts'`
- Mock HubSpot -> null, PB -> >5 rows no name match -> verify `{ resolved: false }`
- All null -> `{ resolved: false }`
- Phone identifier -> calls `findContactByPhone`
- Email identifier -> calls `searchContacts`
- HubSpot ID identifier -> calls `getContact`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `server/lib/identity-resolver.js` exports `resolve(identifier)`
- [ ] Correctly parses phone / email / hubspot-id identifiers
- [ ] HubSpot lookup works for all 3 identifier types
- [ ] PB contacts matching enforces company+name rule (never returns wrong person)
- [ ] Returns `{ resolved: false }` when no match found
- [ ] Ready for Steps 3-4 extension in Bead 9

title: Post-call customer_interactions sync
description:
## Phase A, Bead 4 — Post-Call Sync

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 151-177)

### What
Wire the stripped `interaction-sync.js` module (created in Bead 1) into the call disposition flow so every completed call writes to `customer_interactions`.

### Modify: `server/routes/history.js`

Insert after the HubSpot `.catch()` block (ending ~line 156), before `res.json(call)` at ~line 159. Add fire-and-forget `syncInteraction()`:

```javascript
// After HubSpot .catch(), before res.json(call):
const { syncInteraction } = require('../lib/interaction-sync');
syncInteraction({
  channel: 'voice',
  direction: 'outbound',
  sessionId: `npc_${call.conference_name || call.id}`,  // guard: conference_name has no NOT NULL constraint
  phone: call.lead_phone,
  contactName: call.lead_name,
  companyName: call.lead_company,
  agentName: call.caller_identity,
  recordingUrl: call.recording_url,
  summary: notes || '',
  productsDiscussed: products_discussed || [],
  disposition: qualification === 'hot' ? 'qualified_hot' : qualification === 'warm' ? 'qualified_warm' : disposition,
  // NOTE: `qualification` param is JSONB in customer_interactions — must be object or omitted, NEVER a string.
  // nucleus_phone_calls uses VARCHAR ('hot'/'warm') — do NOT pass that directly.
  qualification: qualification ? { stage: qualification, score: qualification === 'hot' ? 90 : 60 } : undefined,
}).catch(err => console.error('Interaction sync failed:', err.message));
```

### Key Details
- **Fire-and-forget:** `.catch()` prevents sync failure from blocking the disposition response
- **Session ID format:** `npc_` prefix distinguishes nucleus-phone calls from Fireflies (`ff_`) and other channels
- **Qualification mapping:** `nucleus_phone_calls.qualification` is VARCHAR ('hot'/'warm'), but `customer_interactions.qualification` is JSONB -- must transform
- **Upsert behavior:** `interaction-sync.js` uses `ON CONFLICT (session_id, channel)` so re-saving disposition updates rather than duplicates

### Dependencies
- Requires `server/lib/interaction-sync.js` (created in Bead 1: nucleus-phone-5j1.1)
- Requires `customer_interactions` table (exists in shared Postgres, DDL in `joruva-ucil/src/lib/db.js`)

### File Reference
- `server/routes/history.js` — the PUT endpoint for saving call dispositions
- Look for the block that updates `nucleus_phone_calls` and then does HubSpot sync with `.catch()`

### Testing
- Save disposition -> verify row appears in `customer_interactions` with correct session_id format
- Save same disposition twice -> verify upsert (1 row, not 2)
- Verify existing Slack + HubSpot flow unchanged (sync failure does not break response)
- Verify `qualification` field is JSONB object, not string

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `syncInteraction()` call added to history.js after HubSpot block
- [ ] Fire-and-forget pattern (`.catch()` prevents blocking)
- [ ] Session ID uses `npc_` prefix with conference_name fallback
- [ ] Qualification properly mapped from VARCHAR to JSONB
- [ ] Existing disposition flow (Slack, HubSpot) unaffected

title: Cockpit API endpoint
description:
## Phase B, Bead 6 — Cockpit API Endpoint

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 200-233)

### What
Create the cockpit API endpoint that assembles data from 7+ sources and returns a full pre-call briefing. Also mount all 3 new routes in index.js.

### Create: `server/routes/cockpit.js` (~200 lines)

**Endpoints:**

`GET /api/cockpit/:identifier` — data assembly pipeline:
1. `identityResolver.resolve(identifier)` -> ResolvedIdentity
2. Parallel queries via `Promise.all`:
   - `lookupCustomer({phone, email, contactId})` -> interaction history (from `server/lib/customer-lookup.js`)
   - `SELECT ... FROM nucleus_phone_calls WHERE lead_phone = $1` -> prior calls
   - `SELECT ... FROM v35_discovery_queue WHERE domain = $1 OR company_name ILIKE $2` -> pipeline data
   - `SELECT ... FROM v35_lead_reservoir WHERE domain = $1` -> ICP score
   - `SELECT fields_available FROM qa_results WHERE email = $1` -> QA intel
   - `SELECT ... FROM v35_webhook_events WHERE lead_email = $1` -> email engagement
   - If hubspotCompanyId: `getCompany(companyId)` -> company properties
3. `claude.generateRapportIntel(assembled)` -> rapport intelligence
4. Return full cockpit payload

`GET /api/cockpit/:identifier?refresh=true` — bust Claude cache + re-fetch

**Error handling:** Inline `try/catch + res.status(500).json({error})` pattern matching existing `history.js`, `contacts.js`. Do NOT use `next(err)`.

**Auth:** `apiKeyAuth` per-handler (same pattern as contacts.js, history.js).

### Modify: `server/index.js`

Insert between line 44 (`app.use('/api/history', ...)`) and line 46 (`const clientDist = ...`). **MUST go BEFORE `express.static` + catch-all `app.get('*')`.** If placed after, API requests silently return `index.html` with status 200 (no error, extremely hard to debug).

```javascript
// Line 45 (new) — MUST be before `const clientDist` at line 47:
app.use('/api/cockpit', require('./routes/cockpit'));
app.use('/api/fireflies-sync', require('./routes/fireflies-sync'));
app.use('/api/scoreboard', require('./routes/scoreboard'));
```

**Note:** Mount all 3 routes now even though fireflies-sync and scoreboard route files don't exist yet. Create stub files that export an empty router so the server doesn't crash. This prevents future beads from needing to touch index.js.

**Verification:** After implementation, `curl /api/cockpit/test` must return JSON (not HTML). If it returns HTML, routes are in wrong position.

### Dependencies
- Requires `server/lib/identity-resolver.js` (Bead 3: nucleus-phone-5j1.4)
- Requires `server/lib/claude.js` (Bead 5: nucleus-phone-5j1.3)
- Requires `server/lib/customer-lookup.js` (Bead 1)
- Requires `server/lib/hubspot.js` with `getCompany` (Bead 1)

### Known Limitation
In-memory Claude cache is single-process only. If Render scales to multiple instances, each runs its own cache. Fix: move to `ucil_sync_state` with JSONB cache. Not needed at current volume (single instance).

### Testing
- Known HubSpot contact -> full payload with all sections populated
- Unknown phone -> `{ identity: { resolved: false }, rapport: { fallback: true } }`
- `?refresh=true` -> cache busted, Claude called again
- Response time <3s for known contact
- Verify `curl /api/cockpit/test` returns JSON, not HTML

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `server/routes/cockpit.js` exists with GET endpoint
- [ ] All 7+ data sources queried in parallel
- [ ] Claude rapport intelligence integrated
- [ ] `?refresh=true` busts cache
- [ ] All 3 new routes mounted in index.js BEFORE static middleware
- [ ] Stub files for fireflies-sync and scoreboard routes
- [ ] `apiKeyAuth` on all handlers

title: Cockpit client page + components
description:
## Phase B, Bead 7 — Cockpit Client Page + Components

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 237-275)

### What
Build the full cockpit UI: main page, 5 panel components, fetch hook, and wire into routing. Replace Contacts expand with cockpit navigation.

### New Files

| File | Purpose | ~Lines |
|------|---------|--------|
| `client/src/pages/Cockpit.jsx` | Main page: loads data, renders panels, handles actions | 200 |
| `client/src/hooks/useCockpit.js` | Fetch hook with loading/error/refresh + **AbortController cleanup** on unmount | 70 |
| `client/src/components/cockpit/RapportCard.jsx` | Top card: photo, name, headline, rapport starters, opening line | 80 |

| `client/src/components/cockpit/ProductPanel.jsx` | Product reference + watch-outs | 50 |

### Modify Existing Files

**`client/src/lib/api.js`:**
- Add `getCockpit(identifier, signal)` and `refreshCockpit(identifier, signal)`
- Thread `signal` through to `apiFetch` by adding optional `signal` support to `apiFetch(path, options)` (pass `options.signal` to `fetch`)
- This enables AbortController cleanup in `useCockpit`

**`client/src/App.jsx`:**
- Add route with explicit props (matching Contacts pattern):
  ```jsx
  <Route path="/cockpit/:id" element={
    <Cockpit identity={identity} callState={callState} twilioStatus={twilioHook.status} />
  } />
  ```

**`client/src/pages/Contacts.jsx`:**
- **Replace expand entirely:** contact row click -> `navigate(\`/cockpit/${contact.id}\`)`
- Remove `expanded` state and the expand detail section
- Keep green phone button for quick-dial (bypasses cockpit, goes straight to dialer)
- **Exclude TEST_CONTACT** (`_isTest: true`) from cockpit navigation -- keep test contact as quick-dial only

**`client/src/components/layout/Shell.jsx`:**
- No nav change (Cockpit navigated from Contacts, not a tab)

### Layout (mobile-first)
1. Back button + contact name header
2. **RapportCard** (full-width, always first): profile photo, name, title, headline. Rapport starters as colored chips. Opening line in card.
3. **IntelPanel**: nuggets as bullet cards left, adapted script collapsible right (stacks on mobile)

5. **ProductPanel**: product cards + watch-out warnings
6. **Sticky bottom**: "Call Now" (green, full-width) + "Refresh Intel" (secondary)

### Skeleton Loading
Shimmer cards matching each panel shape while API loads.

### Scroll + Filter Persistence
Save both Contacts scroll position AND active filter to `sessionStorage` before navigating to cockpit. Restore on back-navigation. Prevents mobile users losing their place and having filters reset to "All".

### Dependencies

title: Fireflies feedback loop
description:
## Phase C, Bead 8 — Fireflies Feedback Loop

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 280-317)

### What
Build the Fireflies transcript sync system that pulls non-nucleus calls, deduplicates against existing records, analyzes with Claude, and writes to `customer_interactions`.

### Create: `server/config/team.json`
```json
{
  "members": [
    { "name": "Tom", "email": "tom@joruva.com", "identity": "tom", "role": "admin" },
    { "name": "Paul", "email": "paul@joruva.com", "identity": "paul", "role": "admin" },
    { "name": "Kate", "email": "kate@joruva.com", "identity": "kate", "role": "caller" },
    { "name": "Britt", "email": "britt@joruva.com", "identity": "britt", "role": "caller" },
    { "name": "Ryann", "email": "ryann@joruva.com", "identity": "ryann", "role": "caller" },
    { "name": "Alex", "email": "alex@joruva.com", "identity": "alex", "role": "caller" }
  ]
}
```

### Create: `scripts/test-fireflies-query.js`
**PRE-IMPLEMENTATION VALIDATION.** Standalone script that runs the Fireflies `transcripts` GraphQL query and logs actual field names. Verify: is it `date` or `dateTime`? `attendees` or `participants`? Does `sentences` require a separate `transcript(id)` detail query? The existing `server/lib/fireflies.js` only uses upload mutation -- provides zero guidance on query schema. **Run this BEFORE implementing fireflies-sync.js.**

### Create: `server/lib/fireflies-sync.js` (~200 lines)

**Flow:**
1. Read `ucil_sync_state` WHERE `sync_key = 'fireflies'` -> `last_sync_at`
2. Fireflies GraphQL: `{ transcripts(fromDate: ...) { id, title, date, duration, attendees, sentences { ... } } }`
3. Filter: only transcripts where >=1 attendee email matches `team.json` member
4. **Hard cap: max 20 transcripts per sync run.** First run (no last_sync_at): seed with `NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'`
5. For each transcript:
   - **3-layer dedup:**
     (a) Match Fireflies title against nucleus-phone upload pattern (`CNC Call -- {name} at {company} -- {date}`, set by `server/lib/fireflies.js`) -- skip if matches existing `nucleus_phone_calls` record
     (b) Check `customer_interactions` for `session_id LIKE 'npc_%'` matching same phone/timeframe -- prevents double-write
     (c) Fallback: +/-15min timestamp window + matching phone
   - Identify contact via `identityResolver.resolve(attendeePhone)`
   - Claude analysis: sentiment, competitive mentions, products discussed, summary
   - `syncInteraction({ channel: 'voice', direction: 'inbound', sessionId: \`ff_${transcriptId}\`, ... })`
6. Update `ucil_sync_state`

### Create: `server/routes/fireflies-sync.js`
- `POST /api/fireflies-sync` (apiKeyAuth per-handler) -- triggered by n8n cron every 30 min
- Route mount already handled in Bead 6 (stub file will be replaced)

### Dependencies
- Requires `server/lib/interaction-sync.js` (Bead 1: nucleus-phone-5j1.1)
- Requires `server/lib/identity-resolver.js` (Bead 3: nucleus-phone-5j1.4) -- but the resolver is a nice-to-have here, can fall back to raw attendee data if not available
- Requires `ucil_sync_state` table (Bead 2: nucleus-phone-5j1.2)
- Uses existing `FIREFLIES_API_KEY` env var
- Uses `server/lib/claude.js` for transcript analysis (Bead 5, but could use simpler analysis)

### Testing
- Mock Fireflies returning 2 transcripts -> 2 `customer_interactions` rows created
- Dedup test: existing call within +/-5min -> skip
- Dedup test: title matches nucleus-phone upload pattern -> skip
- Fireflies API down -> graceful error, sync_state unchanged
- First run with no prior sync -> seeds with 7-day lookback
- >20 transcripts -> only processes first 20

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `server/config/team.json` created with all 6 team members
- [ ] `scripts/test-fireflies-query.js` validates actual Fireflies GraphQL schema
- [ ] `server/lib/fireflies-sync.js` implements full flow with 3-layer dedup
- [ ] `server/routes/fireflies-sync.js` replaces stub with POST endpoint
- [ ] Hard cap of 20 transcripts per sync run
- [ ] 7-day lookback on first run
- [ ] Graceful error handling (sync_state unchanged on failure)

title: Apollo + Dropcontact identity steps
description:
## Phase C, Bead 9 — Apollo + Dropcontact Identity Extensions

**Plan:** `~/.claude/plans/cheeky-booping-pond.md` (lines 320-359)

### What
Add Steps 3-4 to the identity resolver: Apollo person match and Dropcontact reverse search. These are secondary enrichment -- most contacts resolve at Steps 1-2 (HubSpot + PB). Steps 3-4 cost credits (10/day budget each).

### Create: `server/lib/apollo.js` (~80 lines)
- `matchPerson({name, organization, email})` via Apollo `/api/v1/people/match` (1 credit per call)
- Returns: LinkedIn URL, email, title, or null if no match
- Guard: skip if `APOLLO_API_KEY` env var missing

### Create: `server/lib/dropcontact.js` (~60 lines)
- `reverseSearch({phone, name})` via Dropcontact batch API
- Returns: email, or null
- Guard: skip if `DROPCONTACT_API_KEY` env var missing

### Modify: `server/lib/identity-resolver.js`

Add Steps 3-4 after existing Steps 1-2:
- **Step 3:** Apollo `matchPerson()` if name known but need LinkedIn/email
- **Step 4:** Dropcontact `reverseSearch()` if no email found from Steps 1-3

### Credit Budget Enforcement

Use atomic DB counter in `ucil_sync_state` (table created in Bead 2):

```javascript
async function checkCreditBudget(service, dailyLimit = 10) {
  const syncKey = `${service}_daily_credits`;
  const { rows } = await pool.query(
    `SELECT metadata FROM ucil_sync_state WHERE sync_key = $1`, [syncKey]
  );
  const meta = rows[0]?.metadata || {};
  const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);

  if (meta.date === today && (meta.credits_used || 0) >= dailyLimit) {
    return false; // budget exhausted
  }

  const newUsed = meta.date === today ? (meta.credits_used || 0) + 1 : 1;
  await pool.query(`
    INSERT INTO ucil_sync_state (sync_key, last_sync_at, metadata)
    VALUES ($1, NOW(), $2)
    ON CONFLICT (sync_key) DO UPDATE SET metadata = $2, updated_at = NOW()
  `, [syncKey, JSON.stringify({ date: today, credits_used: newUsed })]);

  return true;
}
```

Same function for both Apollo (`checkCreditBudget('apollo')`) and Dropcontact (`checkCreditBudget('dropcontact')`).

### Env Vars (Phase C -- must add to Render)
- `APOLLO_API_KEY` -- copy from V3.5 `.env`
- `DROPCONTACT_API_KEY` -- copy from V3.5 `.env`

### Dependencies
- Requires `server/lib/identity-resolver.js` (Bead 3: nucleus-phone-5j1.4)

title: Cockpit pre-call briefing is thin for signal-sourced contacts
description:
## Problem
When a rep clicks a signal-scored contact, the cockpit shows name/title/company but the SUGGESTED OPENER and rapport intel are generic ("Hi Logan, this is calling from Joruva Industrial"). The practice call simulator has rich mock data (equipment, pain points, company research, talking points). Real signal contacts get almost nothing.

## Root Cause
The cockpit's generateRapportIntel() in server/lib/claude.js builds the AI briefing from assembled data:
- interactionHistory (customer_interactions) — empty for new contacts
- priorCalls (nucleus_phone_calls) — none, never called
- pipelineData (v35_discovery_queue) — may be empty if not in discovery pipeline
- icpScore (v35_lead_reservoir fit_score) — may be null
- signalMetadata — available when company is in signal pipeline (cert expiry, DoD contracts, etc.)
- emailEngagement (v35_webhook_events) — may be empty
- companyData (HubSpot company) — null for non-HubSpot companies

For signal-sourced contacts, most of these are empty. The AI has almost nothing to work with.

## What Should Happen
The cockpit should leverage the signal data we DO have:
1. **Signal metadata**: cert_expiry_date, cert_standard, contract_total, dod_flag, source_count — these should drive specific talking points ("I see your AS9100 cert is up for renewal in October...")
2. **Company enrichment**: Apollo/Dropcontact returned company details during enrichment — industry, employee count, location. Currently discarded.
3. **LinkedIn profile data**: v35_pb_contacts has linkedin_profile_url, title, industry. The cockpit could scrape/reference this.
4. **Equipment context**: If the company is in our equipment catalog, surface relevant products.

## Files
- server/routes/cockpit.js:127-138 — assembled data object passed to generateRapportIntel
- server/lib/claude.js — generateRapportIntel() prompt construction
- Signal metadata is available in the assembled object as `signalMetadata` (added this session)

## Expected Behavior
A rep clicking a SPEAR-tier contact with cert_expiry Oct 2026 + $1.5M DoD contract should see:
- "Their AS9100 cert expires October 2026 — ask about their recertification timeline"
- "Recent DoD contract ($1.5M) suggests active aerospace manufacturing"
- Company-specific talking points based on industry/size
- Contact-specific talking points based on title (VP Ops → operational efficiency, QA Dir → compliance)

title: Phase 3: Ask Nucleus frontend — chat UI, streaming, escalation button
description:
## Context

Frontend for the Ask Nucleus conversational AI assistant. Renders streaming SSE responses, persists conversation state via server, and provides escalation UX when Claude cannot answer.

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/compiled-bubbling-lark.md — Feature 2 frontend + Feature 3 frontend

Depends on: nucleus-phone-cgz (Phase 2 — backend must be complete: POST /api/ask SSE endpoint, conversation CRUD, escalate endpoint)

## What to Implement

### client/src/pages/AskNucleus.jsx (new file)

Chat UI page:
- Empty state with suggested prompts: "What did my last call with [company] cover?", "JRS-10E specs", "What objections came up this week?"
- Message list: user bubbles (right, accent bg), assistant bubbles (left, card bg)
- Streaming text renders incrementally (append text_delta content as it arrives)
- Tool-use indicator: "Searching calls..." / "Looking up products..." (from tool_status SSE events)
- When done SSE event has escalation field: render a "Send to Tom" action button below the message
- Clicking "Send to Tom" calls escalate function, shows confirmation: "Sent to Tom. He'll see it on his phone."
- Input bar at bottom with Enter-to-send
- Clear history button (calls DELETE endpoint, resets conversationId)

Design: Follow existing Sentinel/Abyss theme from Shell.jsx. Use bg-jv-card, text-jv-text, text-jv-muted, bg-jv-accent patterns.

### client/src/hooks/useAskNucleus.js (new file)

- conversationId state: persists across tab navigation (Shell keeps children mounted via Outlet). Also stored in localStorage for refresh survival.
- On mount: if localStorage has conversationId, verify with lightweight GET /api/ask/conversations/:id — reset to null on 404 (deleted conversation).
- messages state: mirror of server-side conversation, updated from SSE stream
- sendMessage(text): POST to /api/ask with { message, conversationId }, read SSE stream via response.body.getReader(), update messages from stream events, store returned conversationId from done event
- isLoading state
- clearHistory(): calls DELETE /api/ask/conversations/:id endpoint, resets conversationId + messages + localStorage
- escalate(question, context, company, contact): calls POST /api/ask/escalate

### client/src/lib/api.js — Add API functions

IMPORTANT: askNucleus() must use raw fetch(), NOT apiFetch — needs raw Response for SSE streaming (apiFetch calls res.json()). Include credentials: 'include' and X-Requested-With header manually.

- askNucleus(message, conversationId) — raw fetch POST /api/ask, returns Response object
- askNucleusEscalate(data) — uses apiFetch (needs X-Requested-With for CSRF)
- askNucleusConversations() — uses apiFetch
- askNucleusConversation(id) — uses apiFetch
- askNucleusDeleteConversation(id) — uses apiFetch with method: 'DELETE'

### Wiring changes (2 existing files)

- client/src/components/layout/Shell.jsx (line ~19): Add { path: '/ask', label: 'Ask', icon: '💬' } as last tab
- client/src/App.jsx: Import AskNucleus page + add route /ask element

### Updated tab bar order
Contacts | [Live*] | History | Notes | Score | Practice | Ask
*Live = admin only. 7 tabs for admin, 6 for callers.

## Key Files to Read
- client/src/components/layout/Shell.jsx — tab bar structure, theme classes
- client/src/App.jsx — route definitions, how props are passed to pages
- client/src/lib/api.js — apiFetch helper, existing API function patterns
- client/src/hooks/useCallState.js — hook pattern reference
- client/src/pages/History.jsx — page layout reference for Sentinel theme
- client/src/pages/Scoreboard.jsx — card layout reference

## SSE Stream Reading Pattern

The hook reads the SSE stream using response.body.getReader() + TextDecoder. Parse each line for data: prefix, JSON.parse the payload. Handle three event types:
- text_delta: append text to current assistant message
- tool_status: show loading indicator with tool name
- done: finalize message, store conversationId, check for escalation field

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ask tab visible in Shell nav bar (last position)
- [ ] Empty state shows suggested prompts
- [ ] Messages stream incrementally (not all-at-once)
- [ ] Tool-use shows "Searching calls..." indicator
- [ ] "Send to Tom" button appears when escalation field present in done event
- [ ] Clicking escalation button sends notification + shows confirmation
- [ ] Chat history persists across tab navigation (Outlet keeps component mounted)
- [ ] Chat reloads on page refresh (conversationId in localStorage, verified against server)
- [ ] Clear history works (deletes conversation, resets UI)
- [ ] Sentinel/Abyss theme consistent with rest of app

Shipped with Phase 1 (Call Summary/Notes tab), Phase 2 (Ask Nucleus backend with SSE streaming, tool-use, escalation detection), Phase 3 (Ask Nucleus frontend chat UI). title: Production debug mode: server core + instrumentation + API routes
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/synthetic-wondering-mountain.md

During a session on ~April 9, silent Vapi webhook 401s went undetected for 4 days because nucleus-phone has no structured logging, no queryable event trail, and no integration health visibility. This issue implements the entire server side of the production debug system.

## What to implement

### 1. New modules (zero deps on existing code)

**server/lib/debug-log.js** (~50 lines)
- `logEvent(category, source, summary, opts)` gated on `process.env.DEBUG === '1'`
- When off: return immediately, zero cost
- When on: buffer events, batch INSERT into `debug_events` every 2s
- On INSERT failure: `console.warn` + drop buffer (no retry, no unbounded growth)
- Export `flush()` returning a Promise for SIGTERM cleanup

**server/lib/health-tracker.js** (~25 lines)
- In-memory Map of `{ source: lastSeenTimestamp }`
- `touch(source)` called on successful integration calls -- ALWAYS on, not gated on DEBUG
- `getAll()` returns the Map contents for the health endpoint

### 2. Database table

In `server/db.js` `initSchema()` (after the existing tables, ~line 80+), add:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS debug_events (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  category VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  source VARCHAR(60) NOT NULL,
  level VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'info',
  summary TEXT NOT NULL,
  detail JSONB,
  call_id TEXT,
  caller_identity VARCHAR(50)
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_debug_events_ts ON debug_events(ts DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_debug_events_category ON debug_events(category, ts DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_debug_events_call_id ON debug_events(call_id) WHERE call_id IS NOT NULL;
```

### 3. API routes

**server/routes/debug.js** (~120 lines), mounted at `/api/debug` with `apiKeyAuth + rbac('admin')` in server/index.js (line ~77, alongside other admin mounts).

Four endpoints:
- `GET /api/debug/events?category=&level=&callId=&caller=&since=&limit=100` -- query event log
- `GET /api/debug/health` -- integration health (last-seen per source from health-tracker + live DB ping)
- `GET /api/debug/connections` -- active WebSocket subscriptions (import `getConnectionStats()` from live-analysis.js)
- `GET /api/debug/sweep` -- last 20 sweep events

Response shapes are defined in the plan.

### 4. Instrument 13 server files (repetitive pattern)

Each file gets 1-3 lines: `const { logEvent } = require('../lib/debug-log');` (and optionally health-tracker touch). The instrumentation points with categories:

| File | Category | What |
|------|----------|------|
| server/routes/sim.js (webhook entry) | webhook | Silent 401s, missing payloads. `touch('vapi.webhook')` on success |
| server/routes/sim.js (end-of-call) | webhook | Null transcripts, API fallback |
| server/routes/voice.js | webhook | Twilio TwiML arrivals. `touch('twilio.webhook')` |
| server/routes/transcription.js | webhook | RT transcription. `touch('twilio.transcription')` |
| server/routes/apollo-webhook.js | webhook | Apollo phone delivery. `touch('apollo.webhook')`. Replace DEBUG_APOLLO_WEBHOOK |
| server/lib/live-analysis.js | state_change | WebSocket subscribe/disconnect. Also export `getConnectionStats()` |
| server/routes/ask.js | error | Replace DEBUG_ASK_ERRORS |
| server/middleware/error.js | error | Unhandled Express errors |
| server/middleware/rbac.js | error | RBAC 401/403 blocks with blocked URL |
| server/lib/vapi.js | integration | Outbound calls. `touch('vapi.api')` on success |
| server/lib/claude.js | integration | Failures. `touch('anthropic')` on success |
| server/lib/slack.js | integration | Delivery failures. `touch('slack')` on success |

### 5. Prune logic in stale-sweep.js

Add to the end of the sweep cycle:
```sql
DELETE FROM debug_events WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM debug_events ORDER BY ts DESC LIMIT 1000)
```
Plus a `logEvent('sweep', 'stale-sweep', ...)` call for sweep results.

### 6. SIGTERM integration

In server/index.js SIGTERM handler (~line 133): call `await flush()` from debug-log.js BEFORE `await drain(8000)`. Drain consumes the time budget; flush must go first.

### 7. Tests

Create `server/lib/__tests__/debug-log.test.js`:
- No-op when DEBUG unset/off
- Buffer fills and flushes on interval
- Failed INSERT drops buffer and warns

Create `server/routes/__tests__/debug.test.js`:
- Admin-only access (non-admin gets 403)
- Query param filtering works
- Response shapes match plan spec

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `debug_events` table created on startup
- [ ] `logEvent` is no-op when DEBUG !== '1', buffers+batch-inserts when on
- [ ] health-tracker always tracks, regardless of DEBUG
- [ ] All 4 debug API endpoints return correct shapes
- [ ] 13 files instrumented with logEvent/touch calls
- [ ] Prune keeps max 1000 rows
- [ ] SIGTERM flushes before draining
- [ ] Tests pass
close_reason: Completed: production debug mode server core + client UI

title: Investigate Claude web search beta access for equipment auto-lookup
description:
## Problem

The equipment-lookup.js web search fallback (Claude Sonnet with `anthropic-beta: web-search-2025-03-05`) is not populating the equipment_catalog. Tested 2026-03-31 — sightings log correctly but catalog remains empty after web search path runs.

Possible causes:
1. API key lacks web search beta access
2. Web search response format doesn't match expected JSON structure
3. 15s timeout too short for web search + response

## Impact

Without web search fallback, unknown equipment mentioned during calls won't get auto-cataloged. The seed data (nucleus-phone-35s) covers the most common equipment, but edge cases will remain as unresolved sightings until manually added.

## To investigate

- Check if the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on Render has web search beta enabled
- Test web search locally with verbose logging
- If beta access unavailable, consider alternative approaches (e.g., pre-populated lookup via DataForSEO)

## References

- `server/lib/equipment-lookup.js:81-177` (webSearchAndInsert function)
- `server/lib/equipment-pipeline.js` (calls lookupEquipment)

title: Validate Vapi transfer destination format + Slack bot token
description:
## Context

Before writing any production code for the E2E warm transfer feature, we need to validate two unknowns that shape the entire implementation.

Plan: `~/.claude/plans/elegant-dazzling-hammock.md` (Step 0)
Codebase: `/Users/Shared/joruva-ucil/`

## What to implement

Create `scripts/test-vapi-transfer.js` — a throwaway validation script that tests two things:

### A. Vapi `destination` field format

- Configure ONE assistant (Dexter, least-used) with a `transferToAlex` tool via Vapi API (`VAPI_API_KEY`)
- Make a real test call, trigger the transfer
- Log exactly what Vapi sends back in status-update events during/after transfer
- **Critical question:** Does `destination` go at the result-item level (`{ results: [{ toolCallId, result, destination }] }`) or at the response root (`{ results: [...], destination: {...} }`)? Log the raw response Vapi consumes and confirm which shape triggers the PSTN transfer.
- If `destination` doesn't work, document that `transferPlan` on the assistant is the fallback (tool only does notification, no destination field)
- Document findings in a comment at top of the future `onTransferToAlex` handler

### B. Slack bot token + `#joruva-sales`

- Post a test message to `C0ANZR3HLNP` (`#joruva-sales`) using the `claude_mcp` bot token from `~/.joruva/secrets.env` (`SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`)
- Verify `chat:write` scope works for this channel
- Read the `thread_ts` back, post a reply to it
- Confirm both succeed (if not, bot needs to join the channel first via `conversations.join`)
- Also test `C0AQG3HK0AC` (`#joruva-sales-managers`)

### Output

- Document all findings clearly — these findings inform Steps 1-5 of the plan
- Delete the script after validation (or mark it for deletion)
- If Vapi `destination` doesn't work, note the fallback approach in the plan

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Vapi transfer mechanism confirmed (destination field shape documented OR fallback to transferPlan documented)
- [ ] Slack bot can post to both `#joruva-sales` and `#joruva-sales-managers`
- [ ] Slack thread replies work (thread_ts round-trip confirmed)
- [ ] Findings documented for subsequent implementation tasks

## Dependencies

None — this is the first task, blocks everything else.
close_reason: Validated: (1) Slack bot posts+threads work on both #joruva-sales and #joruva-sales-managers, (2) Vapi custom tools can't trigger transfers — use two-tool sequence (custom transferToAlex + built-in transferCall), (3) Dexter configured with both tools via scripts/test-vapi-transfer.js. Plan updated. Live test call pending but not blocking implementation.

title: Harden Date.now() mock in double-cleanup test
description:
Linus review round 6 of nucleus-phone-cas. conversation-pipeline.test.js 'double cleanup' test uses jest.spyOn(Date, 'now').mockReturnValueOnce(after). This works today because the recentlyAborted.has() guard short-circuits before Date.now() is called on the second invocation — but if someone later adds a Date.now() call inside cleanupConversation before the guard, the mock consumes silently and the test still passes for the wrong reason.

Fix: use jest.spyOn(Date, 'now').mockReturnValue(after) (no Once) with a proper afterEach mockRestore(). Or use jest.useFakeTimers({ now: after }).

Also: Date.now.mockRestore?.() optional-chain is unnecessary — drop it.

File: server/lib/__tests__/conversation-pipeline.test.js 'double cleanup' describe block.

title: Backfill phone numbers for SPEAR/TARGETED contacts only
description:
## Context

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 5

## What to do

### Step 1: Count the SPEAR/TARGETED subset

```sql
SELECT c.signal_tier, COUNT(*) 
FROM v35_pb_contacts p
JOIN v35_signal_companies c ON p.domain = c.domain
WHERE p.source = 'apollo' AND p.phone IS NULL
GROUP BY c.signal_tier;
```

Report the count to Tom with credit cost estimate (count * 8 credits). Get approval before proceeding.

### Step 2: Backfill apollo_person_id from cache (free, DB-only)

If apollo_person_id column was added (Phase 2), backfill IDs from v35_apollo_contact_cache where possible. This is a DB-only operation — no API calls, no credit cost.

### Step 3: Run reconciliation for SPEAR/TARGETED only

Use the reconciliation script (from Phase 4) with `--with-phone` flag, filtered to SPEAR/TARGETED tier contacts only. This will re-reveal phones at 8 credits each.

### Step 4: Update CLAUDE.md

Document the apollo_person_id column in the database tables section if not already done.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: nucleus-phone-vtk (webhook matching — apollo_person_id column)
- **Depends on**: nucleus-phone-42n (reconciliation script)

## Acceptance criteria

- SPEAR/TARGETED subset counted and credit cost estimated
- apollo_person_id backfilled from cache where possible

- Results reported: X phones recovered, Y still missing

title: Dedupe React install: client/node_modules shadows root
description:
Jest config currently pins React via moduleNameMapper in jest.client.config.js because client/node_modules/react exists alongside the root node_modules/react. Closer-copy resolution splits React across components (inner) and @testing-library (outer), producing "Cannot read properties of null (reading 'useEffect')" during tests.

The moduleNameMapper fix works but treats the symptom. The root cause is the workspace install layout. Investigate whether to:
- Drop the client-level package.json's react dep and rely on root-hoisted
- Use npm workspaces / yarn workspaces to dedupe
- Pin peer versions

Filed during nucleus-phone-m4k Linus review (issue #13).

Files:
- jest.client.config.js — contains the pinning entries
- client/package.json — has its own react dep
- package.json — root react dep
