title: M1 backend: bearerAuth middleware + history.js per-route composer + secondary sort
description:
## Context

The dialer needs Bearer auth on the three GET history routes while preserving existing cookie/CSRF behavior on `POST /:id/disposition` (web automation depends on it). Per-route middleware composition keeps the surfaces cleanly separated.

Plan: `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md` — see "Backend changes" → `server/middleware/auth.js` and `server/routes/history.js` sections + Architecture decision #2.
Parent epic: `joruva-dialer-mac-vq4` (M1 History tab).
Sibling bead: `nucleus-phone-t3x` (auth exchange route — mints the Bearer JWT this middleware verifies).

## What to implement

### 1. Modify `server/middleware/auth.js`
Add two new middlewares alongside existing `sessionAuth` and `apiKeyAuth`:

- **`bearerAuth(req, res, next)`**:
  - Reads `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header
  - Verifies via existing `jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET)` (same JWT exchange route mints)
  - Calls existing `loadUserById` helper
  - Sets `req.user = { ...user, authSource: 'bearer' }`
  - **No CSRF check.** No cookie fallback.
  - Returns 401 if header missing, malformed, or jwt verify fails

- **`bearerOrSession(req, res, next)`**:
  - `req.headers.authorization ? bearerAuth(req,res,next) : sessionAuth(req,res,next)`
  - Used by the three history GET routes

**CRITICAL — update module exports**:
```js
module.exports = { apiKeyAuth, sessionAuth, bearerAuth, bearerOrSession, loadUserById, invalidateUser, SESSION_TTL_SECONDS, __testSetUser }
```
Without this, `history.js`'s `require('../middleware/auth')` will get `undefined` and fail at first request.

### 2. Modify `server/routes/history.js`
**Per-route middleware, not router-level wrapping.** Replace `sessionAuth` ONLY on the three GET routes the dialer needs:

- `GET /` → `bearerOrSession`
- `GET /:id` → `bearerOrSession`
- `GET /:id/timeline` → `bearerOrSession`

**Do NOT modify `POST /:id/disposition`** — stays on `apiKeyAuth` (which falls back to `sessionAuth`). Web automation depends on the cookie path; bundling all routes under a single composer would break it.

### 3. Add secondary sort to LIST query
Add `, npc.id DESC` as secondary sort to `ORDER BY npc.created_at DESC` (one-line; eliminates pagination flicker on tied timestamps). **LIST query only.**

### 4. Tests `server/routes/__tests__/history.test.js` (extend)
- Add cases for the bearer-auth path (mock JWT verify same way existing tests do, send via `Authorization: Bearer` instead of cookie)
- Cover: non-admin ownership filter still applies under bearer
- **Regression guard**: verify `POST /:id/disposition` is unaffected by the GET-route changes

## Acceptance

- `npx jest server/routes/__tests__/history.test.js` passes
- Live curl with bearer token: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <nucleus_session_token>" '$APP_URL/api/history?limit=5'` → 200 + `{ calls, total }`
- Fake bearer → 401
- Caller-role test user (e.g. kate's) sees only her own calls under bearer
- POST disposition still works via cookie+API-key (no regression)

## References

- Existing `sessionAuth` and `apiKeyAuth`: `server/middleware/auth.js`
- `loadUserById`: `server/middleware/auth.js`
- History routes: `server/routes/history.js` (LIST_COLUMNS lines 18-24, ownership filter pattern lines 201-206)
- Plan file: `/Users/outpost/.claude/plans/snappy-greeting-zebra.md`

## Blocks

- Dialer HistoryListView task — needs Bearer auth working on GET /api/history
close_reason: Implemented bearerAuth + bearerOrSession middleware (server/middleware/auth.js), swapped sessionAuth→bearerOrSession on GET /, GET /:id, GET /:id/timeline in server/routes/history.js, added ', npc.id DESC' tie-break to LIST ORDER BY, extended history.test.js with 11 new bearer cases + ORDER BY assertion + POST disposition regression guard. Linus review surfaced 3 P1 test bugs (bearer-vs-cookie test asserting nothing meaningful, ownership test ambiguity, cache-race on userId 999999) — all fixed. 53/53 history tests pass. Commit 46e7b6d.

title: Equipment search API route + admin review queue
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/giggly-rolling-canyon.md (Phase 8)
Codebase: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/

Admin API for reviewing equipment data, especially unverified auto-inserts from Claude web search. Required to prevent bad specs from generating wrong sizing recommendations. Can run in parallel with pipeline work since it only depends on the DB schema.

## What to implement

### `server/routes/equipment.js` (NEW)

All routes behind `apiKeyAuth` middleware (same as other protected routes in index.js).

Endpoints:
- `GET /api/equipment/search?q=haas` — text search across manufacturer + model (case-insensitive ILIKE)
- `GET /api/equipment/unverified` — all entries with `confidence = 'unverified'` (admin review queue). JOIN equipment_details. Order by created_at DESC.
- `GET /api/equipment/sightings?resolved=false` — unresolved sightings. Order by count DESC, created_at DESC.
- `PUT /api/equipment/:id/verify` — admin marks entry as verified. Sets `confidence = 'high'`, `verified_by = req.user.identity`, `last_verified_at = NOW()`.

### Modify `server/index.js`

Register route:
```js
app.use('/api/equipment', apiKeyAuth, require('./routes/equipment'));
```

### Test considerations

This is a small CRUD route. No unit test file needed — the endpoints are straightforward SQL queries behind auth. Verify manually:
- Search returns results for known seed data
- Unverified queue returns web-search-inserted entries
- Verify endpoint updates confidence + verified_by

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] GET /api/equipment/search?q=haas returns matching equipment
- [ ] GET /api/equipment/unverified returns entries with confidence='unverified'
- [ ] GET /api/equipment/sightings?resolved=false returns unresolved sightings
- [ ] PUT /api/equipment/:id/verify updates confidence to 'high' and sets verified_by
- [ ] All routes require apiKeyAuth
- [ ] Route registered in index.js
- [ ] All existing tests pass: `npm test`

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nucleus-phone-axr (equipment tables must exist)
close_reason: Completed

title: URGENT: Cut DataForSEO daily budget from 5000 to 500 in V3.5 constants.js
description:
## Context

DataForSEO account balance: $42.48 remaining of $401 deposited. $358 spent since Mar 29 (~10 active days = ~$36/day). At current burn rate, account runs dry in ~1 day.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 0

## Resolved Decisions (Tom-approved)

- DataForSEO daily budget: **500 credits/day** (~$7/day). Down from 5,000.
- Apollo daily budget: **50 credits/day**. Down from 400.

## What to change

### File: `/Users/Shared/joruva-v35-scripts/src/lib/constants.js` (line ~213)

```
dataforseo: 5000,   // ~$20/day cap at $0.004/query
```
Change to:
```
dataforseo: 500,    // ~$7/day. Actual cost ~$0.014/query (Live Advanced). Account balance critical.
```

### Same file (line ~209)

```
apollo: 400,        // Pro plan: 6.5K credits/mo
```
Change to:
```
apollo: 50,         // Basic 1K credits/mo. 8 credits per phone reveal = ~6 phone reveals/day.
```

### Investigation (while in V3.5 codebase)

2. Look for DataForSEO `appendix/statistics` endpoint usage or add a note about needing per-endpoint cost investigation

## Important

- This is in the **V3.5 repo** (`/Users/Shared/joruva-v35-scripts/`), NOT nucleus-phone

## Acceptance criteria

- `DAILY_BUDGET.apollo` = 50 in constants.js

title: Auth exchange polish: deferred Linus pass-2 items
description:
Five small cleanup items deferred from the second Linus review of nucleus-phone-t3x (auth exchange route). All non-blocking — safe to batch.

## Items

1. **Delete redundant 'is_active=false' test** in `server/routes/__tests__/auth-exchange.test.js` — currently it mocks the same empty-rows response as the test above it and asserts the same 403. SQL filter `WHERE is_active = TRUE` already excludes the row, so the test proves nothing new. Either delete it or rewrite as a real test against actual rows with `is_active=false`.

2. **Wire debug-log on auth/exchange route** — `server/routes/auth.js` POST /exchange uses `console.warn/error` only. Per `memory/runbooks/nucleus-phone.md`, RBAC events go to `debug_events` table. The next silent auth outage will be invisible until grep'd from Render logs. Add `logEvent('error', 'auth.exchange', ...)` calls for: 401 on verify failure, 403 on missing user, 409 on oid collision/mismatch.

3. **Soften lazy-require comment in `server/lib/entra-token.js`** — comment overstates the cost of adding babel-jest server-side (claims it would 'slow down 800+ tests'; in practice babel-jest is cached after first run). Honest reason is 'I don't want to touch jest config and the lazy-require works.' Trim accordingly.

4. **Tighten kill-switch ordering comment in `server/routes/auth.js`** — current comment claims kill-switch-before-rate-limit is a deliberate ordering choice. In reality any order works because the 503 short-circuits all subsequent middleware. Either tighten to '503 short-circuits before any other middleware' or delete.

5. **Neutralize 503 message in `server/routes/auth.js`** — `{ error: 'Native exchange disabled' }` leaks implementation detail. iOS client doesn't have a graceful fallback path for that string. Change to `{ error: 'Service temporarily unavailable' }` (more generic) or document the contract somewhere the iOS client can rely on.

## Acceptance
- All 5 changes applied
- Tests still pass

## Source
Second Linus review of bead nucleus-phone-t3x, 2026-04-28 session.

title: Mocked module tests: conference, claude, identity-resolver, fireflies-sync
description:

### 1. `server/lib/__tests__/conference.test.js`

title: Apollo/vapi wrapper refactor — Linus nits follow-up
description:
Follow-up nits from Linus review of nucleus-phone-65n (apollo.js wrapper migration). Non-blocking taste/design issues.

Scope:

1. **Status-based triage in signal-enrichment.js** (server/lib/signal-enrichment.js:150-160)
   Current heuristic: `failures.length > contacts.length` → console.error vs console.warn. Arbitrary — 5/5 logs warn, 5/4 logs error. Replace with status-based triage:
   - any 5xx → .error (server-side outage, page-worthy)
   - only 4xx → .warn (client-side issue, includes 429 rate limit — visible but not alerting)
   More useful signal for operators watching logs.

2. **Brittle log-format assertion in apollo.test.js:209**
   Test asserts exact string 'Apollo reveal failed for p2 (status 500)'. When someone reformats the log message this test breaks for no real reason. Either match ID and status independently or drop the log-text assertion entirely — the failures array is the real contract.

3. **Consider per-service fetch helper before next wrapper migrates**
   apollo.js has a verbose pattern: `const endpoint = 'x/y'` declared just to avoid duplicating the string between the URL template and the throwHttpError() call. Five occurrences. Before migrating hubspot.js/twilio.js/fireflies.js/dropcontact.js/claude.js/slack.js, evaluate extracting `fetchApollo(endpoint, body, apiKey)` (or a generic `jsonFetch` factory). Could cut ~30 lines across wrappers. Decide once before the pattern ossifies across 6+ wrappers.

Parent: nucleus-phone-65n. Context: commit migrating apollo.js + vapi.js to shared http-error.js helper (option C collect-and-return for partial reveal failures).
close_reason: All 3 items resolved: status-based triage, brittle assertion loosened, jsonFetch decision made (no factory)

title: Follow-up: strip recording_url from GET /api/history JSON response
description:
## Context

`LIST_COLUMNS` in `server/routes/history.js:18-24` ships `recording_url` (raw Twilio URL with account-SID basic auth) to every API response. Native iOS ignores it (uses signed-URL endpoint), and the web client's link is broken anyway. Tiny win, not blocking M1.

## What to do

1. Remove `recording_url` from `LIST_COLUMNS` in `server/routes/history.js`
2. Verify `recording_duration` is still in the response (that's the "has recording" signal clients use)
3. Update `server/routes/__tests__/history.test.js` to assert `recording_url` is NOT in response
4. Audit `client/src/` for any remaining `recording_url` consumers (the web playback follow-up will replace them with signed-URL fetches anyway, but flag any other reads)

## Acceptance

- Tests pass

title: Database schema + equipment CRUD + lookup modules
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/giggly-rolling-canyon.md (Phases 1-2)
Codebase: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/

First issue in the Equipment Knowledge Base feature chain. Creates the database tables and the core data access modules that everything else depends on.

## What to implement

### 1. Database schema changes — `server/db.js` (144 lines)

Add after the `sim_call_scores` block (line 138), before the final `console.log`:

**A) Try fuzzystrmatch extension** — wrap in try/catch, set module-level `FUZZY_AVAILABLE` flag (exported so equipment-db.js can check it).

**B) Three new tables:**
- `equipment_catalog` — manufacturer, model, model_variants (TEXT[]), category, subcategory, cfm_min/max/typical, psi_required, duty_cycle_pct, air_quality_class, source (CHECK), confidence (CHECK includes 'unverified'), UNIQUE(manufacturer, model), GIN index on model_variants
- `equipment_details` — equipment_id FK, description, typical_applications, industries, air_usage_notes, recommended_compressor/dryer/filters, key_selling_points, common_objections, etc. UNIQUE(equipment_id)
- `equipment_sightings` — raw_mention, count, call_type CHECK('real','practice'), catalog_match_id FK, resolved flag

See plan Phase 1.2 for exact SQL (lines 49-124 of plan).

**C) New columns on nucleus_phone_calls:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE nucleus_phone_calls ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS transcript TEXT;
ALTER TABLE nucleus_phone_calls ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ai_summary TEXT;
ALTER TABLE nucleus_phone_calls ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ai_action_items JSONB;
ALTER TABLE nucleus_phone_calls ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ai_disposition_suggestion VARCHAR(30);
ALTER TABLE nucleus_phone_calls ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ai_summarized BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE;
ALTER TABLE nucleus_phone_calls ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS caller_call_sid VARCHAR(50);
```

### 2. `server/lib/equipment-db.js` (NEW)

CJS module exporting:
- `findByManufacturerModel(mfg, model)` — exact match, case-insensitive, LEFT JOIN equipment_details
- `findByVariant(mfg, modelVariant)` — searches model_variants array with ILIKE ANY
- `findFuzzy(mfg, model)` — uses fuzzystrmatch levenshtein if available, JS fallback otherwise (20-line inline Levenshtein, no npm dep)
- `insertEquipment(catalogData, detailsData)` — INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE, upsert details
- `logSighting(data)` — INSERT INTO equipment_sightings

Pattern: `pool.query()` with parameterized SQL, try/catch, return `{ error: true, message }` on failure. Same pattern as `server/lib/claude.js`.

### 3. `server/lib/equipment-lookup.js` (NEW)

Single export: `lookupEquipment(manufacturer, model)`

Priority chain: exact match -> variant match -> fuzzy match -> Claude web search fallback (raw fetch to Anthropic API with `'anthropic-beta': 'web-search-2025-03-05'` header, 15s timeout) -> auto-insert with `confidence='unverified'`, `source='web_search'`.

**Concurrent dedup:** Module-level Map of in-flight web searches. Check before firing, wait on existing Promise if duplicate. Prevents expensive duplicate web searches.

### 4. Tests — `server/lib/__tests__/equipment-db.test.js`

Unit tests with mock pool:
- Exact match returns catalog + details
- Variant match works
- Fuzzy match (both fuzzystrmatch and JS fallback paths)
- Insert + conflict update
- Sighting insert
- Lookup chain falls through correctly
- Web search result auto-inserted with confidence='unverified'

Existing test pattern: see `server/lib/__tests__/claude.test.js` for mocking style.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Server starts cleanly with new schema
- [ ] `SELECT * FROM equipment_catalog LIMIT 1` returns empty (tables exist)
- [ ] nucleus_phone_calls has transcript, ai_summary, ai_action_items, ai_disposition_suggestion, ai_summarized, caller_call_sid columns
- [ ] FUZZY_AVAILABLE exported from db.js
- [ ] equipment-db.js all 5 functions work (tested)
- [ ] equipment-lookup.js chain falls through correctly (tested)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: `npm test`

## Dependencies

None — this is the first issue in the chain.
close_reason: Completed — DB schema (3 equipment tables + columns) and equipment CRUD/lookup/entity-extractor/sizing-engine modules implemented with 230 passing tests

title: LiveAssistant DirectSaleCTA component + parallel config display
description:
## Context
Plan: ~/.claude/plans/ethereal-wandering-journal.md (Phase 1b)

When the sizing engine recommends a `salesChannel: 'direct'` product, the LiveAssistant UI should show two CTAs instead of a price: "Schedule 10-Min Compressed Air Audit with Alex" (Slack post) and "Send Custom Quote via Email" (POST to /api/quote-request, implemented in next issue). For ecommerce products, behavior is unchanged.

Depends on: catalog + sizing engine issue (salesChannel field must exist on recommendations)

## What to implement

### 1. NEW: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/client/src/components/cockpit/DirectSaleCTA.jsx

Two-button CTA component for direct-sale recommendations. Receives props:
- `recommendation` - full recommendation object (compressor, parallelConfig, dryer, filters, demand, salesChannel)
- `contact` - cockpit contact state (leadName, leadCompany, leadPhone, email from ContactIdentity)
- `callId` - current call ID

**CTA 1: "Schedule 10-Min Compressed Air Audit with Alex"**
- POST to Slack #joruva-sales via existing server endpoint or a new lightweight `/api/quote-request/audit-callback` (TBD -- could also just reuse the Slack webhook pattern from server/lib/slack.js)
- Actually: simpler approach -- POST body to `/api/quote-request` with `type: 'audit_callback'` and let the server handle Slack posting. Single endpoint, two paths.
- Fire-and-forget: if Slack fails, log error but don't block UI. Show success state regardless.
- Include: lead name, company, phone, recommended system model/HP/CFM, full equipment list, call ID

**CTA 2: "Send Custom Quote via Email"**
- Shows email input field (pre-filled from contact state if available)
- Consent checkbox: "I agree to receive a custom quote via email"
- POST to `/api/quote-request` with: { email, consent: true, callId, leadName, leadCompany, leadPhone, recommendation }
- **POST requests MUST include `X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest` header** (sessionAuth CSRF protection, see /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/middleware/auth.js lines 9-12)
- Show success/error states

Component styling: follow existing cockpit patterns -- use CSS vars (--cockpit-live-500, --cockpit-text, etc.). See LiveAnalysis.jsx for reference.

Currently (lines 213-306), the recommendation card always shows price. Add conditional rendering:

- If `recommendation.salesChannel === 'direct'`: render DirectSaleCTA component instead of the price display
- If `recommendation.salesChannel === 'ecommerce'` (or missing for backward compat): render existing price display unchanged
- Parallel config display: when `recommendation.parallelConfig` exists, show "2x JLF-476" format instead of single compressor card. Show parallelConfig.totalCfm as system total.

  - Ecommerce: current layout (model, specs, price)
  - Direct: model + specs + "Direct Sale" badge, then DirectSaleCTA below
  - Parallel: "Nx MODEL" header, combined system specs, DirectSaleCTA (always direct)

title: Frontend LiveAnalysis widget + Cockpit integration
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/giggly-rolling-canyon.md (Phase 7)
Codebase: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/

Frontend React components: WebSocket hook + LiveAnalysis widget + Cockpit.jsx integration + PracticeCallButton callback threading. This is purely frontend work consuming the WebSocket server built in the pipeline issue.

## What to implement

### 1. `client/src/hooks/useLiveAnalysis.js` (NEW)

WebSocket hook:
- Connects to `ws[s]://${location.host}/api/live-analysis` when `enabled=true`
- On open: sends `{ type: 'subscribe', callId }`
- State: `{ equipment: [], sizing: null, recommendation: null, connected: false }`
- Accumulates equipment detections (deduped by manufacturer+model)
- Auto-reconnect on close (3s delay, max 5 retries)
- Cleanup on unmount or callId change
- Protocol: ws:// for localhost, wss:// for production (check `location.protocol`)

```js
export default function useLiveAnalysis(callId, enabled = true) { ... }
```

### 2. `client/src/components/cockpit/LiveAnalysis.jsx` (NEW)

Blue accent color (`--cockpit-blue-*` tokens already exist in `client/src/cockpit-theme.css`).

Progressive rendering phases:
1. **Listening** (no equipment): Subtle blue-bordered card, pulsing blue dot, "Listening for equipment..."
2. **Equipment detected**: List with manufacturer/model badges, CFM per machine
3. **Demand estimated**: Aggregated CFM/PSI summary bar
4. **Recommendation ready**: CAS product card with model, price, CFM, reasoning. Show "pricing available on request" for null prices.

Pulsing border animation on new data (CSS `@keyframes` with blue glow).

**Warning icon** for unverified equipment (confidence === 'unverified' from web search auto-insert).

### 3. Modify `client/src/pages/Cockpit.jsx` (185 lines)

Add LiveAnalysis to left column after `<ProductReference />`:

```jsx
import LiveAnalysis from '../components/cockpit/LiveAnalysis';
import useLiveAnalysis from '../hooks/useLiveAnalysis';

// Derive callId for WebSocket subscription:
const [activeSimCallId, setActiveSimCallId] = useState(null);
const liveCallId = isPractice
  ? (activeSimCallId ? `sim-${activeSimCallId}` : null)
  : callState.callData?.conferenceName;
const liveAnalysis = useLiveAnalysis(liveCallId, callPhase === 'active' || activeSimCallId);

// In JSX, after ProductReference:
<LiveAnalysis data={liveAnalysis} />
```

Thread `onCallStart` callback through PracticeCallButton:
```jsx
<PracticeCallButton
  identity={identity}
  onScoreComplete={handleScoreComplete}
  onCallStart={(simId) => setActiveSimCallId(simId)}
  onCallEnd={() => setActiveSimCallId(null)}
/>
```

### 4. Modify `client/src/components/cockpit/PracticeCallButton.jsx` (379 lines)

Add `onCallStart` and `onCallEnd` props. Fire `onCallStart(simCallId)` after successful call initiation (~line 130 where the call starts). Fire `onCallEnd()` when call completes/cancels/errors.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] useLiveAnalysis hook connects to WebSocket, subscribes, accumulates state
- [ ] Auto-reconnect works (3s delay, max 5 retries)
- [ ] Cleanup on unmount (no leaks)
- [ ] LiveAnalysis.jsx renders all 4 progressive phases
- [ ] Blue accent colors from existing cockpit-theme.css tokens
- [ ] Null prices show "pricing available on request"
- [ ] Unverified equipment shows warning icon
- [ ] Cockpit.jsx correctly derives callId for both practice and real calls
- [ ] PracticeCallButton fires onCallStart/onCallEnd callbacks
- [ ] Client builds cleanly: `cd client && npm run build`
- [ ] All existing tests pass: `npm test`

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nucleus-phone-e19 (WebSocket server at /api/live-analysis must exist)

title: Extract compressor catalog + expand sizing engine with parallel config and salesChannel
description:
## Context
Plan: ~/.claude/plans/ethereal-wandering-journal.md (Phase 1a, part 1)

The sizing engine currently caps at JRS-25E (25HP, 100 CFM) with 6 catalog entries. CAS manufactures units from 5HP to 476HP across 7 product lines. This issue extracts the catalog into its own module and expands the sizing engine with parallel configuration support and sales channel routing.

## What to implement

### 1. NEW: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/compressor-catalog.js
Extract COMPRESSOR_CATALOG from sizing-engine.js into a standalone module with ~35 entries across all CAS product lines. Include new fields on every entry: `productLine` (rs_open | rs_enclosed | pm_vsd | large_frame | duplex | g_series), `salesChannel` (ecommerce | direct), `pricingStatus` (confirmed | pending | quote_required).

Product lines and verified specs (from plan):
- RS Open Frame 5-25HP: existing 6 entries, keep current prices, add new fields. salesChannel: ecommerce
- RS Open Frame 30-100HP: JRS-30 (125 CFM), JRS-40 (155), JRS-50 (185), JRS-60 (210), JRS-75 (285), JRS-100 (405 -- NOT 500). All 460V/3ph, salesChannel: direct, price: null, pricingStatus: quote_required
- RS Enclosed: JRSE-40 through JRSE-100. CFM identical to open frame 5-60HP, JRSE-75=295 CFM, JRSE-100=405. All direct
- PM VSD: JVSD-10 (34), JVSD-15 (53), JVSD-20 (71), JVSD-30 (109), JVSD-50 (187), JVSD-60 (230), JVSD-75 (320), JVSD-100 (406), JVSD-125 (577), JVSD-150 (741). All direct
- Large Frame (two-stage preferred for sizing): JLF-125 (518), JLF-150 (653), JLF-180 (749), JLF-220 (875), JLF-270 (1121), JLF-335 (1369), JLF-425 (1670), JLF-476 (1895). NO 200HP model. All direct
- Duplex: JDX-5 (32) through JDX-40 (310). All direct
- G-Series: JGS-10, JGS-25, JGS-30. CFM identical to RS at same HP. All direct (CAS authorization unclear)

**All catalog arrays MUST be sorted by CFM ascending.** The sizing engine uses `.find()` for first-fit.

Export: `module.exports = { COMPRESSOR_CATALOG }`

### 2. MODIFY: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/sizing-engine.js

**a) Import catalog**: Replace inline COMPRESSOR_CATALOG (lines 12-19) with `require('./compressor-catalog')`.

**b) Expand dryer catalog** (line 22-28): Add JRD-200 (200 CFM), JRD-500 (500 CFM), JRD-1000 (1000 CFM), JRD-2000 (2000 CFM). All with `price: null, pricingStatus: 'quote_required'`. Keep existing 5 entries unchanged. Must remain sorted by CFM.

**c) Expand filter catalog** (lines 40-49): Add JPF-500 (500 CFM, price null, quote_required), JPF-1000 (1000 CFM), JCF-500 (500 CFM), JCF-1000 (1000 CFM). Append AFTER existing entries so indices [0] and [1] don't change. **CRITICAL**: Do NOT change the FILTER_CATALOG alias (lines 52-55) -- it's hardcoded to index [1] and must continue pointing to JPF-130/JCF-130.

**d) Expand desiccant catalog** (lines 33-36): Add JDD-200 (200 CFM) and JDD-500 (500 CFM), both quote_required. Append after existing entries.

**e) Add parallel configuration logic** to recommendSystem() (line 111-146):
- When demand.adjustedCfm > largest catalog entry's CFM, compute N = ceil(adjustedCfm / largestUnit.cfm)
- Build parallelConfig: { units: [N copies of largest], totalCfm: N * largest.cfm, unitCount: N, configuration: 'parallel' }
- `compressor` field still points to the single unit (contract from plan)
- Remove the old "exceeds largest unit" note when parallelConfig is populated
- Dryer sizing in parallel configs uses parallelConfig.totalCfm (the dryer must cover full system output)
- Desiccant CFM selection in parallel configs uses single compressor CFM (each compressor has its own treatment loop)

**f) Add deriveSalesChannel(recommendation) function**:
- Scans compressor + dryer + filters + desiccantUpgrade (if present)
- If ANY component has salesChannel: 'direct' OR pricingStatus: 'quote_required', top-level salesChannel = 'direct'
- Sets recommendation.salesChannel and recommendation.pricingStatus
- Must be called AFTER addQualityFilters() so desiccant upgrade is included

**g) Add salesChannel/pricingStatus to dryer, filter, and desiccant catalog entries** where missing. Existing dryer/filter/desiccant entries up to 130 CFM: salesChannel: 'ecommerce', pricingStatus: 'confirmed'. New larger entries: salesChannel: 'direct', pricingStatus: 'quote_required'.

**h) Export** deriveSalesChannel from module.exports.

### Ecommerce/direct boundary
- Compressors: 25HP (JRS-25E, 100 CFM) is last ecommerce SKU. 30HP+ is direct.
- Dryers: up to JRD-100 (100 CFM) is ecommerce. 200+ is direct.
- Filters: up to 130 CFM is ecommerce. 500+ is direct.
- Desiccant: JDD-40 and JDD-80 are ecommerce. 200+ is direct.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] compressor-catalog.js exports sorted array with ~35 entries, all required fields present
- [ ] sizing-engine.js imports from compressor-catalog.js
- [ ] recommendSystem() returns parallelConfig for demand > largest single unit
- [ ] Dryer/filter/desiccant catalogs expanded with new entries, sorted by CFM
- [ ] FILTER_CATALOG alias unchanged (still points to JPF-130/JCF-130)
- [ ] deriveSalesChannel() correctly computes worst-case across all components
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (except selectFilter fallback -- expected breakage, fixed in next issue)
- [ ] `npm test` passes
close_reason: Completed in single session

title: Quote request API endpoint + DB migration + Slack notification
description:
## Context
Plan: ~/.claude/plans/ethereal-wandering-journal.md (Phase 1c)

The DirectSaleCTA component (previous issue) POSTs to `/api/quote-request`. This issue implements the server-side handler: DB migration, route handler, Slack notification, and route registration.

Depends on: DirectSaleCTA UI issue (the UI sends requests to this endpoint)

## What to implement

### 1. NEW: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/migrations/003_quote_requests.sql

Create migrations directory if needed (currently doesn't exist). SQL from plan:

```sql
CREATE TABLE quote_requests (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  call_id TEXT,
  lead_email TEXT NOT NULL,
  lead_name TEXT,
  lead_company TEXT,
  lead_phone TEXT,
  recommendation_snapshot JSONB NOT NULL,
  equipment_snapshot JSONB,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
  slack_notified BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_quote_requests_status ON quote_requests(status);

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN NEW.updated_at = NOW(); RETURN NEW; END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_quote_requests_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON quote_requests
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
```

Also add the CREATE TABLE to /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/db.js `initSchema()` (line 17+) so the table is created on startup (same pattern as nucleus_phone_calls). Use `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`. The trigger function and trigger should also be in initSchema.

### 2. NEW: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/quote-request.js

Express router module. Session-authenticated (same cookie auth as WebSocket -- rep is already logged into cockpit).

**POST /api/quote-request** handler:
1. Validate request body:
   - `email`: required, valid email format (simple regex is fine)
   - `consent`: must be `true`
   - `callId`: optional but recommended
   - `leadName`, `leadCompany`, `leadPhone`: optional, pre-filled from cockpit
   - `recommendation`: required JSONB, the full recommendation snapshot
   - `type`: optional, 'quote' (default) or 'audit_callback'
2. Store in `quote_requests` table via pg pool from /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/db.js
3. Post Slack notification to #joruva-sales (use existing `sendSlackAlert` from /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/slack.js):
   - Include: lead name, company, phone, email, recommended system (model/HP/CFM), call ID
   - For `type: 'audit_callback'`: message says "Alex — please call back [lead] at [phone] re: [model] system"
   - For `type: 'quote'`: message says "Quote requested by [lead] ([email]) for [model] system"
4. If Slack post fails: still store the record, set slack_notified=false, return success with `slackFailed: true` warning
5. Return `{ success: true, quoteRequestId: id }` (and optionally `slackFailed: true`)

Auth: use `sessionAuth` middleware (not apiKeyAuth). Import from /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/middleware/auth.js.

### 3. MODIFY: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/index.js

Register the new route. Add after the existing route registrations (around line 56):

```js
app.use('/api/quote-request', require('./routes/quote-request'));
```

Wait -- looking at the pattern, routes are in /server/routes/ but the handler logic can be inline in the route file or in /server/lib/. Given the existing patterns (routes/ files are thin Express routers), create:

- /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/routes/quote-request.js -- Express router with sessionAuth middleware, calls handler in lib
- /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/quote-request.js -- Business logic (DB insert, Slack notification)

Register in index.js: `app.use('/api/quote-request', require('./routes/quote-request'));`
Note: Do NOT wrap with apiKeyAuth -- the route file applies sessionAuth internally.

### 4. Tests

Add /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/routes/__tests__/quote-request.test.js (or server/lib/__tests__/quote-request.test.js depending on existing test patterns):
- Valid POST returns 200 with quoteRequestId
- Missing email returns 400
- Missing consent returns 400
- Invalid email format returns 400
- Missing recommendation returns 400
- Slack failure still returns success with slackFailed warning
- Session auth required (no cookie = 401)

## Key files to read for context

- /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/middleware/auth.js (sessionAuth)
- /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/slack.js (sendSlackAlert)

title: recentlyAborted TTL-on-write cleanup for high-churn dialers
description:
Linus review round 5 of nucleus-phone-cas flagged recentlyAborted Map growth under call churn. Entries only evicted by the 10-minute SWEEP_INTERVAL, so at high volume (dialer hammering through a list) entries pile up for up to 30 min × calls/hour. A few hundred calls/hour is fine; thousands = a few MB of dead Map entries.

Fix: TTL-on-write pattern. When cleanupConversation sets recentlyAborted.set(callId, ts), schedule a setTimeout(STALE_THRESHOLD, () => recentlyAborted.delete(callId)).unref() at the same time. Eliminates the sweep-dependent growth window.

File: server/lib/conversation-pipeline.js cleanupConversation + sweepInterval.

title: Conversation Navigator: Tier 2 question bypass + Tier 0/1 speculative layers
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/declarative-crunching-wadler.md (Phase 2, Epic 2)
Depends on: nucleus-phone-zv4 (Tier 3 backend pipeline must be working first)

## What to do

Add the speed layers on top of the Tier 3 batched pipeline. Tier 2 is straightforward and always valuable. Tiers 0-1 are conditional on validation data from nucleus-phone-ns9 — if cached Haiku latency is consistently <2s, Tiers 0-1 may not be needed. Build Tier 2 first, then decide.

### Tier 2: Question bypass (~2-2.5s)

In `server/lib/conversation-pipeline.js`, modify `processConversationChunk()`:

1. Add question detection regex: check for `?` character, question words at start of sentences (who/what/when/where/why/how/is/are/do/does/can/will/would/could/should), rising intonation patterns
2. When question detected: flush buffer immediately, call Haiku with prompt caching (bypass the 8s timer)
3. Concurrency guard: if `analysisInFlight` is true when question detected, skip the flush (the in-flight call will cover the same buffer content)
4. Log suggestion source as `source: 'haiku'` vs `source: 'haiku_bypass'` to distinguish Tier 2 from Tier 3

### Tier 1: Phase bank (<100ms)

When Haiku returns a phase change, it also returns `phase_bank: [{ trigger, text }]` — 3-5 pre-written suggestions appropriate for the new phase.

1. Add `phase_bank` field to Haiku prompt (already in the response schema from nucleus-phone-zv4, may just need to be populated)
2. When phase changes, broadcast `phase_bank_loaded` message with the bank
3. On incoming chunks, before checking buffer threshold, check if any phase_bank trigger matches the text
4. If match: broadcast `response_suggestion` with `source: 'phase_bank'` immediately (no Haiku call)
5. Clear used phase_bank entry (don't re-match)

### Tier 0: Speculative prediction (0ms)

Haiku returns `predicted_next: { pattern, suggestion }` — a prediction of the next likely question/objection.

1. Add `predicted_next` field to Haiku prompt
2. When Haiku returns a prediction, broadcast `prediction_loaded` to client (stored silently, not rendered)
3. On incoming chunks, check if `prediction.pattern` appears in the text
4. If match: broadcast the pre-computed suggestion with `source: 'prediction'` immediately
5. Clear prediction after match (one-shot)
6. Clear prediction on each new Haiku cycle (stale predictions are worse than no predictions)

### Server-side hit rate logging

Add logging to track which tier serves each suggestion:
```
conversation-navigator: suggestion served | callId=X source=prediction|phase_bank|haiku_bypass|haiku latency=Xms
```

### Unit tests

Add to `server/lib/__tests__/conversation-pipeline.test.js`:
- Question detection regex matches expected patterns
- Question bypass flushes buffer immediately
- Concurrency guard prevents double Haiku calls
- Phase bank matching (trigger in text → instant suggestion)
- Prediction matching (pattern in text → instant suggestion)
- Prediction cleared after match
- Prediction cleared on new Haiku cycle

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Question detection triggers immediate Haiku call (Tier 2)
- [ ] Concurrency guard prevents overlapping Haiku calls
- [ ] Phase bank suggestions served instantly on trigger match (Tier 1)
- [ ] Speculative predictions broadcast and matched on incoming text (Tier 0)
- [ ] Suggestion source logged for hit-rate analysis
- [ ] All unit tests passing
close_reason: Completed: Tier 2 question bypass + source tagging (tier3_batch/tier2_question/exit_assist) + hit-rate logging + stale prediction clearing + abort/zombie state prevention + pattern-equality prediction dedupe. 70 pipeline tests, 789 server tests passing. Six rounds of Linus review addressed. Follow-ups filed: 0cj, 67m, bs8, 9i0, 7qh, ccj.

title: Add full-flow dedupe test (processConversationChunk path) for prediction_loaded
description: Linus review round 6 of nucleus-phone-cas noted the current dedupe test (prediction dedupe describe block) calls handleAnalysisResult directly. Belt-and-suspenders: add a test that drives two processConversationChunk cycles end-to-end via mocked fetch, each returning the same predicted_next.pattern, and asserts only one prediction_loaded broadcast across the two cycles. Proves the full pipeline respects dedupe, not just the leaf function.\n\nFile: server/lib/__tests__/conversation-pipeline.test.js.

title: Phase 2: Ask Nucleus backend — AI chat with tool-use, SSE streaming, escalation
description:
## Context

Ask Nucleus is a conversational AI assistant for sales reps. Claude answers questions about calls, products, and company history using DB-backed tools. Server owns conversation state. SSE streaming to client. Escalation to Tom when Claude cannot answer.

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/compiled-bubbling-lark.md — Features 2 + 3 (Ask Nucleus backend + Escalation)

Depends on: nucleus-phone-fpd (Phase 1 — GIN index + ask_nucleus_conversations table must exist)

## What to Implement

### server/lib/ask-nucleus.js (new file) — Core AI orchestration

System prompt (with prompt caching via cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }):
- User identity + role context
- PRODUCT_CATALOG from server/lib/claude.js line 14 (import or copy the constant)
- Study guide content: read client/public/study-guide.html at startup, strip to text
- Compressor catalog data from server/lib/compressor-catalog.js (full specs, sizing logic)
- Escalation instruction: Claude includes <!--ESCALATE:{"question":"...","context":"..."}--> marker when it cannot answer
- Tone: helpful, concise, sales-oriented

4 tool definitions:

| Tool | Query Pattern | Access Control |
|------|--------------|----------------|
| search_my_calls | to_tsvector/@@  on nucleus_phone_calls (reuses GIN index from Phase 1) | WHERE caller_identity = identity always; admin can override with optional caller param |
| search_interactions | ILIKE on customer_interactions summary/contact_name/company_name | WHERE agent_name = identity for callers; no filter for admins |
| get_product_specs | In-memory lookup from compressor-catalog.js | Everyone |
| get_company_history | All interactions with a company | Filtered by agent_name for callers; unfiltered for admins |

All tools: parameterized SQL, results capped at 10 rows, summaries truncated to 200 chars.

Streaming orchestration:
1. Fetch/create conversation from ask_nucleus_conversations table
2. Auto-rotate at 100 messages (create new conversation, link to previous)
3. Atomic-append user message: UPDATE ... SET messages = messages || $1::jsonb
4. Build Anthropic request: cached system prompt + last 20 messages
5. Call Anthropic with stream: true, 60s AbortController timeout
6. req.on('close') — abort stream on client disconnect
7. Parse Anthropic SSE: content_block_start, content_block_delta, content_block_stop, message_delta
8. Forward text_delta as SSE to client
9. If stop_reason === 'tool_use': pause SSE, execute tool, make non-streaming Anthropic call with tool_result. Repeat up to 5 rounds.
10. Only first turn and final text turn are streamed to client (keeps protocol simple)
11. On completion: regex for escalation marker, strip from text, append clean text to DB, send done SSE event

SSE event format: {"type":"text_delta","text":"..."}, {"type":"tool_status","name":"..."}, {"type":"done","conversationId":123,"escalation":null|{question,context}}

Reference: server/lib/call-summarizer.js for Claude raw fetch + streaming patterns. Model: claude-sonnet-4-6 (same as server/lib/claude.js line 7).

### server/routes/ask.js (new file) — Express Router, sessionAuth

- POST /api/ask — Send message, get SSE stream. Body: { message, conversationId? }. Response: Content-Type: text/event-stream. Headers: Cache-Control: no-cache, X-Accel-Buffering: no (Render proxy).
- GET /api/ask/conversations — List user conversations (id, caller_identity, created_at, updated_at, message_count — no messages field). Non-admin filtered to own.
- GET /api/ask/conversations/:id — Single conversation with messages. Non-admin: WHERE id = $1 AND caller_identity = $2.
- DELETE /api/ask/conversations/:id — Atomic ownership check delete. Non-admin: DELETE ... WHERE id = $1 AND caller_identity = $2.
- POST /api/ask/escalate — Body: { question, context, company?, contact?, conversationId? }. Calls escalateToTom(). Returns { sent: true, channels: ['sms', 'slack'] } or { error: 'rate_limited', retryAfterSeconds: N }.

### server/lib/escalation.js (new file) — Twilio SMS + Slack DM

async function escalateToTom({ repName, question, context, company, contact })
  - rate-limit check (in-memory Map, 1 per rep per 5 min)
  - sends Twilio SMS + Slack DM in parallel
  - returns { sms: bool, slack: bool }

- SMS: client.messages.create({ to: process.env.PHONE_TOM, from: process.env.TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER, body }) — use Twilio client from server/lib/twilio.js
- Slack: sendSlackDM(process.env.TOM_SLACK_USER_ID, text, blocks) — from server/lib/slack.js line 209
- Rate limit: in-memory Map<repIdentity, lastEscalatedAt>, 1 per rep per 5 min

### Wiring: server/index.js
Add: app.use('/api/ask', sessionAuth, require('./routes/ask')) (after quote-request mount ~line 56)

### Env var needed: TOM_SLACK_USER_ID
Already have PHONE_TOM, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER on Render. Only TOM_SLACK_USER_ID needs adding. Do NOT hardcode the value — reference env var only.

### Tests: server/routes/__tests__/ask.test.js (new file)

Key test cases:
- Requires session auth (API key returns 401)
- POST /api/ask creates conversation if no conversationId
- POST /api/ask appends to existing conversation
- SSE response format correct (Content-Type, headers)
- Tool execution respects access control
- Anthropic API errors — graceful error SSE event
- Escalation marker detected, stripped from saved text, included in done event
- Malformed escalation marker — no escalation field, no error
- POST /api/ask/escalate sends SMS + Slack DM
- POST /api/ask/escalate rate-limited (1 per rep per 5 min)
- Partial failure (SMS ok, Slack fail) — returns sent: true with channels array
- DELETE /api/ask/conversations/:id — own only, admin can delete any
- Conversation CRUD list/get endpoints

## Key Files to Read
- server/lib/claude.js — PRODUCT_CATALOG constant (line 14), MODEL (line 7), Anthropic API pattern
- server/lib/call-summarizer.js — Claude raw fetch + streaming reference
- server/lib/compressor-catalog.js — Product data for get_product_specs tool
- server/lib/customer-lookup.js — Query patterns for customer_interactions
- server/lib/slack.js — sendSlackDM() at line 209
- server/lib/twilio.js — Twilio client for SMS
- server/middleware/auth.js — sessionAuth export
- server/db.js — ask_nucleus_conversations table schema (created in Phase 1)
- server/routes/__tests__/history.test.js — test patterns
- client/public/study-guide.html — study guide content for system prompt

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /api/ask returns SSE stream with text deltas and done event
- [ ] Tool-use loop works (Claude calls tool, server executes, continues, up to 5 rounds)
- [ ] Conversation state persisted in ask_nucleus_conversations
- [ ] Auto-rotate at 100 messages
- [ ] Escalation marker detected server-side, stripped from saved text, included in done event
- [ ] POST /api/ask/escalate sends SMS via Twilio + Slack DM, rate-limited 1/rep/5min
- [ ] 60s timeout on Anthropic stream, abort on client disconnect
- [ ] All test cases passing
close_reason: Completed and Linus-approved across 3 review rounds per phase. All 635 tests pass, client builds clean. Shipped with Phase 1 (Call Summary/Notes tab), Phase 2 (Ask Nucleus backend with SSE streaming, tool-use, escalation detection), Phase 3 (Ask Nucleus frontend chat UI). Follow-up bead nucleus-phone-qy6 tracks TOM_SLACK_USER_ID env var setup on Render.

title: Webhook transferToAlex handler + post-transfer tracking + knowledge compiler
description:
## Context

Core implementation: wire the transferToAlex tool into the webhook handler, add post-transfer tracking in end-of-call, and update the knowledge compiler's transfer instructions.

Plan: `~/.claude/plans/elegant-dazzling-hammock.md` (Steps 3-5)
Codebase: `/Users/Shared/joruva-ucil/`

## What to implement

### 1. Modify `handleToolCall` map wrapper — `src/routes/vapi-webhook.js` (line 74-98)

The existing `Promise.all` map wraps every tool result as `{ toolCallId, result: JSON.stringify(result) }`, stripping extra fields. For `transferToAlex`, the `destination` field must pass through to Vapi.

Modify the map callback (line 97):
```javascript
const entry = { toolCallId, result: JSON.stringify(result) };
// Pass through destination for transfer tools
if (result?.destination) {
  entry.destination = result.destination;
  entry.result = JSON.stringify(result.transferResult || { success: true });
}
return entry;
```

**Note:** The exact `destination` field placement (result-item level vs response root) depends on findings from nucleus-phone-7bf. Adjust accordingly.

### 2. Add `transferToAlex` case — `src/routes/vapi-webhook.js` (line 82-91)

Add to the tool call switch:
```javascript
case 'transferToAlex':
  result = await onTransferToAlex(callId, args, message);
  break;
```

### 3. Implement `onTransferToAlex` handler

Flow:
1. `getOrCreateCall(callId)` — ensure call record exists
2. **Await** Slack notification via `notifyTransferToAlex(context)` (from `src/lib/sales-notify.js`) — catch errors, don't crash
3. Write `transfer_status=initiated` + `slack_thread_ts` in ONE query
4. Fire-and-forget: `syncInteraction(...)` with `disposition: 'transfer_to_sales'`
5. Return result with `destination` field for Vapi PSTN transfer:

```javascript
// Sanitize LLM args for TTS safety
const sanitize = (s, max) => (s || '').replace(/[^\w\s.,!?'-]/g, '').slice(0, max);
const safeName = sanitize(str(args.callerName, 'callerName'), 50) || 'unknown';
const safeSummary = sanitize(str(args.conversationSummary, 'conversationSummary'), 200);

return {
  transferResult: { success: true, message: "Connecting to Alex now." },
  destination: {
    type: "number",
    number: process.env.ALEX_PHONE_NUMBER || "+19515286164",
    message: \`Incoming Joruva call. Caller: \${safeName}. \${safeSummary}\`
  }
};
```

**New require at top of file:** `const { notifyTransferToAlex, updateTransferOutcome } = require('../lib/sales-notify');`

### 4. Post-transfer tracking — `handleEndOfCall` (line 125)

After existing logic in `handleEndOfCall`, add transfer outcome tracking:
- Query `transfer_status` and `slack_thread_ts` for the call
- If transferred: update `transfer_status` to `completed` (or `no_answer` if duration < 15s heuristic)
- If `slack_thread_ts` exists: fire-and-forget `updateTransferOutcome(threadTs, outcome)` to both Slack channels

### 5. Knowledge compiler update — `src/lib/knowledge-compiler.js`

In `buildVoicePrompt` (line 222-233, the "Transfer to Alex" section), update instructions to tell the assistant to call the `transferToAlex` tool BEFORE speaking the handoff. The tool triggers both the Slack notification and the actual PSTN transfer.

Current text (line 232): "When transferring: 'Let me connect you with Alex...'"
New text should instruct: call `transferToAlex` with callerName, company, productsDiscussed, currentSetup, sizingData, conversationSummary, qualificationLevel, urgencyIndicators. The tool handles notification and routing.

### 6. Tests — update `tests/vapi-webhook.test.js`

Add test cases (follow existing patterns in the file):
- `transferToAlex` happy path: all args → DB update + Slack awaited + destination in response
- `transferToAlex` minimal args: only `conversationSummary` → still succeeds
- `transferToAlex` Slack failure: non-fatal (mock throws, tool still returns success with destination)
- `transferToAlex` summary sanitization: strips control chars, truncates to 200 chars
- `handleEndOfCall` with transfer: reads slack_thread_ts, posts thread update
- Response format: `destination` field passes through the map wrapper correctly

Mock `sales-notify.js` the same way existing tests mock `sync-worker` and `interaction-sync` — require first, replace exports.

## Existing code references

- Webhook handler: `src/routes/vapi-webhook.js` (entire file, 291 lines)
- `str()` helper: `src/routes/vapi-webhook.js` line 202-215 (reuse for arg coercion)
- `getOrCreateCall`: `src/routes/vapi-webhook.js` line 278-289 (reuse)
- `syncInteraction`: `src/lib/interaction-sync.js` (fire-and-forget pattern at line 169-185)
- Knowledge compiler: `src/lib/knowledge-compiler.js` — `buildVoicePrompt` function, line 54-246
- Existing tests: `tests/vapi-webhook.test.js` — 552 lines, comprehensive patterns to follow

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `transferToAlex` tool call returns `{ transferResult, destination }` with correct shape
- [ ] Map wrapper passes `destination` through to Vapi response
- [ ] Slack notification awaited before returning (non-fatal on failure)
- [ ] DB updated with `transfer_status=initiated`, `slack_thread_ts`, `transfer_context`
- [ ] `handleEndOfCall` updates transfer status + posts Slack thread reply
- [ ] Knowledge compiler prompt instructs assistant to call `transferToAlex` tool
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass: `node --test tests/vapi-webhook.test.js`
- [ ] Summary sanitization strips control chars, truncates to 200 chars

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nucleus-phone-7bf (validation — confirms destination field shape)
- Depends on: nucleus-phone-oe5 (DB migration + sales-notify module must exist)
close_reason: Already completed in commit 8480de7 on joruva-ucil

title: Strip ?u= and ?t= from access logs / Sentry breadcrumbs
description:
Filed during nucleus-phone-op7 Linus review. Recording stream URLs include `?t=<hmac>&exp=<unix>&u=<userId>`. The userId is an internal numeric id (no PII), but it WILL leak through Render access logs, Sentry breadcrumbs, browser history if pasted into a webview, and Slack URL unfurls. The HMAC binding makes it inert for forgery, but it's still an identifier in URLs.

## What to do

- Render: configure access log scrubbing for query params `u` and `t` on `/api/recording/*/stream` paths
- Sentry (if/when integrated): add beforeBreadcrumb hook that strips matching query params
- Document the convention so future signed-URL endpoints follow the same scrubbing

## References

- `server/routes/recording.js` — signed URL format
- nucleus-phone-op7 (Linus review P1 finding)

title: P0: 12 jest failures in call.test.js — POST /api/call/status returns 400 instead of 204
description:
All 12 tests in 'POST /api/call/status' test suite (server/routes/__tests__/call.test.js) fail with 'expected 204, got 400 Bad Request'. Existing tests at session-start commit becabaa, so this is pre-existing technical debt — NOT introduced by 2026-04-27 IVR work.

Failing assertions:
- returns 204 for any event
- does nothing when conference not found
- saves ConferenceSid and dials lead on conference-start
- dials lead on participant-join (races conference-start)
- skips lead dial when claimLeadDial returns false
- skips SID update when conferenceSid already set
- skips dial when leadPhone is null
- returns 204 even when Twilio dial fails
- tracks participant on join
- removes participant on leave
- updates DB and removes conference on end
- removes conference even when DB update fails

Pattern: handler returns 400 where test expects 204. Likely root causes (in order of probability):
1. Twilio webhook signature validation middleware rejecting test fixtures (test env missing the right NODE_ENV / X-Twilio-Signature stub) — this is the same NODE_ENV-validation pattern flagged in nucleus-phone-ndg
2. Body validation added since test was last updated and test fixtures are stale
3. Express 4→5 middleware change

Repro: `cd /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone && npx jest server/routes/__tests__/call.test.js`
Diagnosed during 2026-04-27 land-the-plane after IVR session — found pre-existing on session-start commit.

title: Cockpit priorCalls silent gap for email-only contacts
description:
server/routes/cockpit.js:56-65 only runs the priorCalls query when resolved identity has a phone number. An email-only contact (e.g. resolved via HubSpot email search) with prior calls logged by email_lead will return priorCalls: [] and LastCallCard renders null — silently hiding call history. Pre-existing gap, made more visible by the 0qj/0az work since LastCallCard is now the primary 'you've talked before' surface.

Fix: query nucleus_phone_calls with OR on lead_email when identity has email but no phone. Preserve existing behavior when phone is present. Add regression test.

Caught by Linus review during 0qj/0az second pass.
close_reason: Fixed: priorCalls query now matches on lead_phone OR lead_email

title: Entity extractor + sizing engine modules
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/giggly-rolling-canyon.md (Phase 3)
Codebase: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/

Two new standalone modules: Claude Haiku entity detection from transcript chunks, and a deterministic sizing/recommendation engine. These are pure modules with no server integration yet (that happens in the real-time pipeline issue).

## What to implement

### 1. `server/lib/entity-extractor.js` (NEW)

Claude Haiku (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`) entity detection from transcript chunks.

**Pre-filter regex** (runs BEFORE any API call):
```
/haas|mazak|bridgeport|fanuc|cnc|grizzly|piston|recip|rotary|dryer|booth|blast|erect|pearson|packaging|doosan|okuma|hurco|kitamura|dmg|mori|laguna|thermwood|shopbot|wexxar|loveshaw|hvlp|sander/i
```
- No regex match -> return `[]` immediately (no API cost)
- Match -> call Claude Haiku with 5s timeout
- System prompt: extract equipment mentions, return JSON array `[{ manufacturer, model, count, raw_mention }]`
- Parse with try/catch, clamp, strip markdown fences (same pattern as `server/lib/sim-scorer.js`)

**Also:** Update `server/lib/sim-scorer.js` to use pinned model ID `claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514` instead of unpinned `claude-sonnet-4-6` (consistency per plan).

### 2. `server/lib/sizing-engine.js` (NEW)

Pure deterministic math. No API calls.

Exports:
- `calculateDemand(equipmentList)` — Sum `cfm_typical * duty_cycle_pct/100 * count` with 1.25 safety factor. Returns `{ totalCfmAtDuty, peakCfm, maxPsi, equipmentCount }`
- `recommendSystem(demand)` — Decision tree:
  - CFM <= 28: JRS-7.5E ($7,495)
  - CFM <= 42: JRS-10E ($9,495)
  - CFM <= 60: JRS-15E (price: null)
  - CFM <= 80: JRS-20E (price: null)
  - CFM <= 100: JRS-25E (price: null)
  - Always recommend dryer for CNC shops (price: null). Add filters based on air_quality_class.
  - Returns `{ compressor: { model, hp, cfm, price: number|null }, dryer: { model, price: number|null }, filters[], notes }`

### 3. Tests

**`server/lib/__tests__/entity-extractor.test.js`:**
- Pre-filter skips non-equipment text (no API call)
- Pre-filter passes equipment text
- Claude response parsing (valid JSON, markdown fences, invalid JSON)
- Timeout handling

**`server/lib/__tests__/sizing-engine.test.js`:**
- Zero equipment -> null recommendation
- Single Haas VF-2 -> JRS-7.5E or JRS-10E
- Five CNC machines -> JRS-20E or JRS-25E
- Safety factor (1.25x) applied correctly
- Dryer always recommended for CNC
- Null prices handled correctly

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] entity-extractor.js pre-filter works (no API call for non-equipment text)
- [ ] entity-extractor.js correctly parses Claude Haiku response
- [ ] sizing-engine.js calculateDemand returns correct CFM with safety factor
- [ ] sizing-engine.js recommendSystem maps CFM to correct CAS products
- [ ] sim-scorer.js updated to pinned model ID
- [ ] All tests pass: `npm test`

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nucleus-phone-axr (needs equipment_catalog schema for type understanding, though these modules don't directly query DB)
close_reason: Completed — DB schema (3 equipment tables + columns) and equipment CRUD/lookup/entity-extractor/sizing-engine modules implemented with 230 passing tests

title: Real-time pipeline: WebSocket server + Twilio RT transcription webhook
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/giggly-rolling-canyon.md (Phase 4, parts 4.1-4.5)
Codebase: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/

The real-time pipeline for REAL calls: WebSocket server for pushing live analysis to the frontend, Twilio Real-Time Transcription webhook for receiving transcript chunks, and voice.js modifications to enable RT transcription.

Vapi practice call integration is a separate issue to keep integration surface manageable.

## What to implement

### 1. Install `ws` dependency

```bash
cd /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone && npm install ws
```

### 2. `server/lib/live-analysis.js` (NEW)

WebSocket server module:
- `attachWebSocket(httpServer)` — called from server/index.js
- On `upgrade` to path `/api/live-analysis`: **manually parse** raw `Cookie` header (Express middleware doesn't run on WebSocket upgrade). Extract `nucleus_session`, call `jwt.verify()` with `JWT_SECRET`. Accept/reject.
- State: `Map<callId, Set<ws>>` for subscriptions
- In-memory dedup: `Map<callId, Set<'mfg:model'>>` to avoid re-broadcasting same equipment
- Server->client messages:
  - `{ type: 'equipment_detected', data: { manufacturer, model, count, specs, catalogMatch } }`
  - `{ type: 'sizing_updated', data: { totalCfm, peakCfm, maxPsi, equipmentCount } }`
  - `{ type: 'recommendation_ready', data: { compressor, dryer, filters, notes } }`
  - `{ type: 'transcript_chunk', data: { text, speaker } }`
- Client->server: `{ type: 'subscribe', callId }` / `{ type: 'unsubscribe' }`
- Export `broadcast(callId, message)` for other modules

### 3. Modify `server/index.js` (75 lines)

Capture HTTP server from `app.listen()`:
```js
const httpServer = app.listen(PORT, () => { ... });
const { attachWebSocket } = require('./lib/live-analysis');
attachWebSocket(httpServer);
```

Register new route:
```js
app.use('/api/transcription', require('./routes/transcription'));
```

### 4. `server/routes/transcription.js` (NEW)

Twilio RT Transcription webhook: `POST /api/transcription`

- Validate Twilio signature (same pattern as voice.js line 9-12)
- Receive partial transcript chunks
- Accumulate transcript: `UPDATE nucleus_phone_calls SET transcript = COALESCE(transcript, '') || $1 WHERE caller_call_sid = $2`
- **CallSid mapping:** Query `SELECT id, conference_name FROM nucleus_phone_calls WHERE caller_call_sid = $1` (indexed, < 1ms). Do NOT use in-memory cache (Render restarts lose it).
- Pre-filter with equipment keyword regex
- If keywords match: entity extractor -> equipment lookup -> sizing engine -> broadcast via WebSocket
- Log sightings to equipment_sightings (call_type = 'real')
- Broadcast `transcript_chunk` for real-time display
- **Speaker filtering:** Twilio labels tracks as `inbound_track`/`outbound_track`. Filter entity extraction to lead's track. If ambiguous, process both and tag `speaker: 'unknown'`.

### 5. Modify `server/routes/voice.js` (49 lines)

**A) Save caller's CallSid** (before TwiML generation in default "initiate" branch, ~line 33):
```js
const { pool } = require('../db');  // voice.js currently has no DB access
await pool.query(
  'UPDATE nucleus_phone_calls SET caller_call_sid = $1 WHERE conference_name = $2',
  [req.body.CallSid, ConferenceName]
);
```

**B) Add `<Start><Transcription>`** in default "initiate" branch ONLY (before existing `const dial = twiml.dial(...)` on line 34):
```js
const start = twiml.start();
start.transcription({
  statusCallbackUrl: `${baseUrl}/api/transcription`,
  track: 'both_tracks',
  languageCode: 'en-US',
});
```

IMPORTANT: `twiml.start()` and `twiml.dial()` are SIBLING root-level TwiML verbs. start() BEFORE dial(), NOT nested.

**Note:** voice.js handler is currently sync. Adding the pool.query makes it async. Change `(req, res) =>` to `async (req, res) =>` and add try/catch.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ws` in package.json dependencies
- [ ] WebSocket server accepts connections at /api/live-analysis with JWT auth
- [ ] subscribe/unsubscribe works, broadcast sends to subscribed clients only
- [ ] Twilio RT webhook receives transcript chunks, accumulates in DB
- [ ] Equipment keyword pre-filter runs on transcript chunks
- [ ] Entity extraction -> lookup -> sizing -> broadcast pipeline works end-to-end
- [ ] voice.js saves caller_call_sid and adds <Start><Transcription> TwiML
- [ ] Server starts cleanly
- [ ] All existing tests pass: `npm test`

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nucleus-phone-axr (DB schema: caller_call_sid column, equipment tables)
- Depends on: nucleus-phone-dyk (entity-extractor.js, sizing-engine.js modules)
close_reason: Completed: WebSocket server, Twilio RT transcription webhook, voice.js modifications, all 5 review rounds clean

title: Fix secrets.env syntax for safe shell sourcing
Harden Nucleus Phone access controls before onboarding external users
description:
## Context

Tom mentioned in the Britt call (Apr 9) that Nucleus Phone is 'still really vulnerable' — he can't truly limit access for outside users yet. With Blake Zweigle (Toyota sales manager, Britt's friend) potentially joining as a commission-based caller, this needs to be addressed before granting him access.

## Requirements

- Implement role-based access control (RBAC) for Nucleus Phone users
- External callers should ONLY see: call list, cockpit, call history, practice mode
- External callers should NOT have access to: source code, admin panels, system configuration, database, Reactor internals
- Audit current attack surface: what can a logged-in user access/export/download?
- Add session management: ability to revoke access instantly

## References

- Nucleus Phone repo: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/
- Stardust Nucleus (parent): /Users/Shared/stardust-nucleus/ — has RBAC already (admin/operator roles)
- Tom's concern: 'I don't want anybody getting their hands on the source code'

## Priority

P1 — blocks onboarding Blake Zweigle and any other external callers. Nucleus Phone must exit beta before external access.
close_reason: RBAC deployed and verified on Render. DB users seeded (7 users, 2 admin, 5 caller). Admin CRUD protected (401 without auth). OAuth login flow redirects correctly. Topic firewall and session management operational.

title: Remove duplicate sessionAuth in ask.js route handlers
close_reason: Removed 5 duplicate sessionAuth calls from ask.js route handlers

title: Equipment pipeline integration + comprehensive sizing engine test suite
description:
## Context
Plan: ~/.claude/plans/ethereal-wandering-journal.md (Phase 1a, part 2)

After the catalog expansion and sizing engine changes (previous issue), the equipment-pipeline needs to call addQualityFilters() and deriveSalesChannel() before broadcasting recommendations. The test suite also needs comprehensive expansion covering all new product lines, parallel configs, and boundary conditions.

## What to implement

### 1. MODIFY: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/equipment-pipeline.js

Currently (lines 82-91), after `recommendSystem(demand)`, the pipeline broadcasts the raw recommendation without quality filters or sales channel derivation. This must change:

```
// Current flow (lines 82-91):
if (sizingChanged) {
  const demand = calculateDemand(accumulated);
  broadcast(callId, { type: 'sizing_updated', data: demand });
  const recommendation = recommendSystem(demand);
  if (recommendation) {
    broadcast(callId, { type: 'recommendation_ready', data: recommendation });
  }
}
```

New flow:
1. Import `addQualityFilters` and `deriveSalesChannel` from sizing-engine.js (line 3)
2. After `recommendSystem(demand)`, derive `airQualityClass` from accumulated equipment using priority order: `ISO_8573_1 > paint_grade > general` (take highest-priority class across all items, default null if all are general)
3. Call `addQualityFilters(recommendation, airQualityClass)`
4. Call `deriveSalesChannel(recommendation)` to set top-level salesChannel and pricingStatus
5. Then broadcast `recommendation_ready` with the fully-decorated recommendation

The `accumulated` array already has `air_quality_class` on each item (line 73-74). Just scan it for the highest-priority class.

### 2. MODIFY: /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/__tests__/sizing-engine.test.js

**Expected breakages to fix first:**
- `selectFilter('particulate', 200).model` currently expects `'JPF-130'` (line 245) -- now returns `'JPF-500'` due to expanded catalog. Update to `'JPF-500'`.
- Same for any coalescing filter fallback tests.

**New test categories to add:**

a) **Catalog integrity tests:**
- COMPRESSOR_CATALOG sorted by CFM ascending (update existing test for larger catalog)
- No duplicate models
- Every entry has: model, hp, cfm, psi, voltage, productLine, salesChannel, pricingStatus
- All salesChannel values are 'ecommerce' or 'direct'
- All pricingStatus values are 'confirmed', 'pending', or 'quote_required'
- Ecommerce boundary: all entries with hp <= 25 AND productLine rs_open have salesChannel ecommerce

b) **Sizing scenarios from plan's test matrix:**
- Hobby garage: 3x small mills @ 8 CFM, 50% duty -> ~15 adjusted -> JRS-5E, ecommerce
- Small job shop: 5x Haas VF-2 @ 12 CFM, 60% duty -> ~45 adjusted -> JRS-10E, ecommerce
- Mid job shop: 10x CNC @ 12 CFM, 70% duty -> ~105 adjusted -> JRS-25E, ecommerce
- Growing shop (boundary): 10x CNC @ 12 CFM, 80% duty -> ~120 adjusted -> JRS-30, **direct**
- Growing shop: 15x CNC @ 12 CFM, 70% duty -> ~158 -> JRS-40, **direct**
- Production floor: 25x CNC + 5x grinders -> ~350 -> JRS-75, **direct**
- Large mfg plant: 50x mixed -> ~750 -> JLF-125 or nearby, **direct**
- Mega facility: 100+ machines -> ~1500+ -> parallel config (2x JLF-476), **direct**

c) **Boundary tests:**
- 100 CFM demand -> JRS-25E (ecommerce) vs 101 CFM -> JRS-30 (direct)
- Demand at exactly JLF-476 CFM (1895) -> single unit, no parallel
- Demand at 1896 CFM -> parallel (2x JLF-476)

d) **Parallel config tests:**
- parallelConfig present when demand > largest single unit
- parallelConfig.unitCount = ceil(demand / largest.cfm)
- compressor field still present (points to single unit)
- Always salesChannel: 'direct'
- Dryer sized to parallelConfig.totalCfm, not single unit

e) **salesChannel derivation tests:**
- Small ecommerce system: all components ecommerce -> recommendation.salesChannel = 'ecommerce'
- Direct compressor but ecommerce dryer -> salesChannel = 'direct'
- Ecommerce compressor but desiccant upgrade -> salesChannel = 'direct' (after addQualityFilters)
- deriveSalesChannel computed after addQualityFilters (desiccant included)

f) **Filter sizing for expanded catalog:**
- selectFilter('particulate', 200) -> JPF-500
- selectFilter('particulate', 600) -> JPF-1000
- selectFilter('particulate', 1500) -> JPF-1000 (largest fallback)

g) **Dryer sizing for expanded catalog:**
- Demand requiring JRS-100 (405 CFM) -> dryer JRD-500
- Demand in parallel config (3790 CFM) -> dryer JRD-2000

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] equipment-pipeline.js derives airQualityClass from accumulated equipment
- [ ] equipment-pipeline.js calls addQualityFilters + deriveSalesChannel before broadcasting
- [ ] All existing tests pass (with updated selectFilter expectations)
- [ ] New catalog integrity tests pass
- [ ] All 8 sizing scenarios from plan test matrix pass
- [ ] Boundary tests (ecommerce/direct, parallel trigger) pass
- [ ] Parallel config tests pass
- [ ] salesChannel derivation tests pass
- [ ] `npm test` passes with no failures

title: equipment-db.js insertEquipment() missing 3 equipment_details columns
description:
**Problem**: `insertEquipment()` in `server/lib/equipment-db.js:156-182` only inserts 11 of 13 `equipment_details` columns. Missing: `key_selling_points`, `common_objections`, `recommended_air_quality`.

**Impact**: Equipment added via the live API or web-search auto-insert path silently drops these fields (NULL). The seed script (`scripts/seed-equipment-specs.js:619`) handles all 13 correctly — only the runtime path is affected.

**Fix**: Add the 3 missing columns to the INSERT statement in `insertEquipment()` and the corresponding ON CONFLICT UPDATE SET clause. Update the test in `equipment-db.test.js` to verify round-trip of all fields.

Flagged by Linus code review on 2026-04-02.

title: Migrate jv-* cockpit tokens to aunshin-* tokens (broader cockpit-color rebrand)
description:
## Context

The aunshin-rebrand PR (#1) deliberately scoped to the Login surface + ReactorContainer rename and intentionally preserved legacy 'jv-*' Sentinel cosmology tokens in tailwind.config.js so the cockpit UI doesn't shift in production.

This bead tracks the broader cockpit-color rebrand: migrate every cockpit screen from 'jv-*' Sentinel-cosmology tokens to 'aunshin-*' Twilight-register tokens.

## Current jv-* surface area (post-PR-1)

Approximate sites in client/src/ that still use jv-* tokens:
- ActiveCalls.jsx (3 sites with jv-violet — sim badges, sim indicator dot, sim-mode button)
- Cockpit.jsx (uses jv-bg, jv-card, jv-elevated, jv-amber, jv-bone, jv-pewter)
- Activity.jsx (DISP_BAR, DISP_PILL maps using jv-green, jv-amber, jv-red)
- CallComplete.jsx (DISPOSITIONS array)
- History views, navigator, sentiment arc, equipment widget, qualscript, etc.

Run 'cd /Users/Shared/nucleus-phone && grep -rn -E "\bjv-(bg|card|elevated|border|muted|amber|amber-deep|violet|violet-deep|crown|bone|pewter)" client/src/' for the full inventory.

## Token mapping

See /Users/Shared/nucleus-brand/aunshin-brand-kit/migrations/README.md for the full Sentinel/Reactor → Aunshin token table. Summary:
- jv-bg #06050F → --color-aunshin-twilight #2D1F1C
- jv-card #0F0D29 → --color-aunshin-twilight-2 #4A3631
- jv-amber #F59E0B → --color-aunshin-sodium #F2B86A
- jv-amber-deep #D97706 → --color-aunshin-sodium-deep #D89945
- jv-violet/violetDeep/crown/voidEdge → REMOVED (no Aunshin equivalent — see per-call-site decision rule in migrations/README.md)
- jv-bone #F5F5F4 → --color-aunshin-peach-light #EFD1AF
- jv-pewter #78716C → --color-aunshin-quiet-d #C99A78

## Approach

Plan-mode first. Per-screen migration with visual QA — Render auto-deploys on push to main, so coordinate with the dialer team for each merge window.

## Risks

- Cockpit's deep-blue/violet aesthetic shifts to warm peach/twilight. Significant UX change — buyers/operators will notice.
- 'jv-*' tokens stay alongside 'aunshin-*' during migration; once a screen is fully migrated, audit for unused jv-* tokens and prune them.

## Why deferred from PR #1

PR #1 scope was the Login surface (visible Aunshin entry point) + ReactorContainer→LiveContainer rename + token availability. The full cockpit cascade has UX implications that warrant its own review window and scheduled deploys.

title: Add circuit breaker to hub resolver fallback path (P1)
description:
## Problem

`server/lib/identity-resolver.js:203-224` falls back to inline when the hub is unreachable but does NOT cache the degraded result. During a sustained hub outage, each WebSocket poll on a live call (~5s cadence × N concurrent calls) re-tries the hub every time. That's a self-DDoS against a service we already know is down.

## Fix direction

Per-process circuit breaker:
- Track consecutive failures in a sliding window (use existing `fallbackCounter`).
- After 3 consecutive failures within 30s → OPEN circuit for 30s (skip hub, go straight to inline).
- After 30s → HALF-OPEN (one trial call; success closes, failure re-opens).
- Expose circuit state via `fallbackMetrics()` for /health.

## Discovered

Linus review round-2 of Phase 2c, 2026-04-15 (P1). Not blocking today (hub is up) but guaranteed to bite on the first outage.

title: Add Customer Service line to IVR menu
description:
Add a third option to the master-line IVR (server/routes/main-line.js) for customer service. Per Tom's request 2026-04-27 when the Investor option was added.

- Press 2 → investor relations (rings Tom's cell, no recording, voicemail with Slack + SMS)

Need to add:

Open questions to resolve before implementing:
- Who does customer service ring? (queue of reps? single owner? voicemail-first?)
- Should customer service calls go through the full conference pipeline (recording, transcription, UCIL sync) or be a separate minimal flow like investor.js?
- After-hours behavior?

Implementation will mirror the investor flow if we want it minimal: new server/routes/customer-service.js + new branch in main-line.js menu handler. Update greeting copy to include 'or press 3 for customer service'.

description:
Linus code review (2026-04-25 session) flagged the Twilio Auth Token as compromised: it was pasted as a literal string in /Users/Shared/resist-needlepoint mu-plugin source on the production server (resistneedlepoint.com) for ~2 hours before being relocated to wp-config.php constants. Coordinated rotation completed via Twilio Secondary Auth Token API.

Consumers updated:
1. nucleus-phone Render service (srv-d72rkt1r0fns73afe99g) — env var TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN
2. UCIL Render service (srv-d72bfkma2pns73etavfg) — env var TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN

4. ~/.joruva/secrets.env local — TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN

Procedure:

End-to-end SMS test from local sent successfully with new token. Total elapsed ~7 min, zero customer-facing impact (rotation window kept both tokens valid until promote).

title: Tier 0 prediction matching: normalize whitespace/punctuation
description:
In useLiveAnalysis.js transcript_chunk handler, Tier 0 prediction matching uses text.toLowerCase().includes(pattern.toLowerCase()).

Current limitations:
- ASR output with double spaces ('how  much') won't match pattern 'how much'
- Hyphens/punctuation variants miss ('how-much' vs 'how much')
- Incidental whitespace in author-written patterns (' how much ') may match too loosely via .includes

Today this is fine because server-side patterns are short keywords ('cost', 'warranty'). If patterns get sloppier or multi-word, add normalization: collapse whitespace, strip punctuation, then substring-match.

Filed from nucleus-phone-m4k Linus second-pass review (issue #4). Not urgent.

title: Extend hubspotFetch retry policy to 5xx with jitter+cap
description:
Currently hubspotFetch retries only on 429 (see server/lib/hubspot.js:21-34). 5xx responses (502, 503, 504) are textbook transient failures — LB bounces, pod restarts, Cloudflare hiccups — and failing them immediately on CRM writes loses deals.

## Scope
- Extend retry loop in server/lib/hubspot.js to also retry 5xx with exponential backoff + jitter, capped at MAX_RETRIES (currently 3).
- Keep 4xx (other than 429) as immediate throws — those are client-side issues.
- Update contract test at server/lib/__tests__/hubspot.test.js 'retry-5xx-test' (currently pins 'does NOT retry' — flip it when policy changes). Search for 'TODO(nucleus-phone-RETRY_5XX)'.
- Same treatment likely wanted for other wrappers after 65n completes — consider extracting a retry helper if 2+ wrappers need it.

## Context
Linus review of nucleus-phone-65n hubspot migration flagged this. Parent: nucleus-phone-65n.

title: Signal Engine → Phone Integration
description:
Integrate the signal-amplified outbound engine (v35_signal_metadata scoring ~4,785 companies into SPEAR/TARGETED/AWARENESS tiers) into nucleus-phone so the team gets prioritized, actionable call lists grouped by company, ordered by signal score, with phone-number enrichment for top tiers.

**Repos involved**:

- joruva-multichannel: `/Users/Shared/joruva-multichannel/` (Phase 2 only, TS ESM)

title: Phase 1: Fix Pipeline tab — direct V3.5 query
description:
## Summary
Replace the broken multichannel HTTP proxy in the Pipeline tab with a direct V3.5 Postgres query. The Pipeline tab is currently empty because it expects `signal_tier`, `signal_score`, `cert_expiry_date`, `contract_total`, `source_count` but the multichannel API returns `intent_tier`, `intent_score`, `contact_count`, `personas_covered` — wrong fields entirely.

**This is a temporary fix.** After Phase 2 ships (multichannel API fix), this direct query gets replaced with the corrected API call. Mark code with `// TODO(phase-2-cleanup): replace with MC API after Phase 2 ships`.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/toasty-knitting-marshmallow.md` — Phase 1

## What to change

**`/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/routes/signals.js`** (lines 14-35):
- Replace `GET /pipeline` handler: swap `fetch(MC_BASE/admin/abm/accounts)` with `pool.query()` joining `v35_signal_metadata` + `v35_lead_reservoir`
- Import `pool` from `../db`
- Query:
```sql
SELECT lr.domain, lr.company_name, lr.geo_state,
       lr.enrichment_status, lr.harvest_priority_score,
       sm.signal_tier, sm.signal_score, sm.source_count,
       sm.cert_expiry_date, sm.cert_standard, sm.contract_total, sm.dod_flag
FROM v35_signal_metadata sm
JOIN v35_lead_reservoir lr ON lr.domain = sm.domain
WHERE 1=1
  [AND sm.signal_tier = $tier]
  [AND lr.geo_state = $state]
ORDER BY sm.signal_score DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT $limit OFFSET $offset
```
- Keep response shape `{ companies: [...] }` unchanged so Pipeline.jsx needs no changes

**`/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/client/src/pages/Pipeline.jsx`** — No changes needed (verify it renders correctly with new data)

## Acceptance criteria
- Pipeline tab shows ~4,785 companies with tier badges, scores, cert expiry, contract values
- Filter by SPEAR/TARGETED/AWARENESS works
- Filter by state works
- Code marked with `// TODO(phase-2-cleanup)` comment
