title: Fix webhook matching: apollo_person_id column + match-order improvement + failure logging
description:
## Context

The Apollo phone webhook handler matches contacts by email, then falls back to exact name match. Both strategies have gaps: emails may differ, names may not match exactly. Adding apollo_person_id as the primary match key is far more reliable. Additionally, 1,065 contacts have phone=NULL due to silent match failures — this issue adds comprehensive logging.

**Plan**: `~/.claude/plans/splendid-prancing-papert.md` — Phase 2

**CONDITIONAL**: This issue's scope depends on Phase 0b results (nucleus-phone-1jc). If webhook payloads do NOT contain person.id, skip the apollo_person_id column and focus only on improving email/name matching + failure logging.

## What to change

### DB Migration (run against shared Postgres)

```sql
ALTER TABLE v35_pb_contacts ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS apollo_person_id TEXT;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pb_contacts_apollo_id ON v35_pb_contacts(apollo_person_id) WHERE apollo_person_id IS NOT NULL;
```

### File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/lib/signal-enrichment.js`

In the INSERT into v35_pb_contacts (find the INSERT statement in runBatchEnrichment), add `apollo_person_id` column. The value comes from `revealPerson()` return object (added in nucleus-phone-2xv).

Handle race condition: webhook may arrive before INSERT commits. Strategy: accept the loss — reconciliation script (Phase 4) catches it on its next run. No pending queue needed.

### File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/server/routes/apollo-webhook.js`

Rewrite match order (currently lines 54-75):
1. Match by `apollo_person_id` first (most reliable)
2. Fall back to email match
3. Fall back to name match

On `updated === 0`: log full payload as warning with all match attempts that failed and why.

### File: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/CLAUDE.md`

Document the new `apollo_person_id` column in the database tables section.

## Dependencies

- **Blocked by**: nucleus-phone-1jc (webhook payload logging — need to confirm payload shape first)
- **Depends on**: nucleus-phone-2xv (credit accounting — adds apollo_person_id to revealPerson return)

## Acceptance criteria

- `apollo_person_id` column exists on v35_pb_contacts with partial index
- Webhook handler matches by apollo_person_id first, then email, then name
- All webhook attempts logged (success and failure) with enough detail to debug
- CLAUDE.md updated with new column documentation
close_reason: Completed: apollo_person_id column added, webhook payload parsing fixed ({ people: [] } not { person: {} }), matching by apollo_person_id, 1516 contacts backfilled, end-to-end test passed. 4 rounds of code review clean.

title: Reduce sim stuck-row guard window from 10min to 3min
description: The 10-minute guard in server/routes/sim.js:186-194 blocks new practice calls when a prior row is stuck in 'in-progress'. Combined with the stale-sweep (also 10min, runs every 5min), a failed call can lock a rep out for up to 15 minutes. The PracticeCallButton.jsx fix (39e9227) now auto-cancels on client failure, but server-side the window is still too long for edge cases (browser crash, network drop). Reduce SIM_STALE_MINUTES in stale-sweep.js:15 and the INTERVAL in sim.js:189 from 10 to 3 minutes. Also reduce sweep interval from 5min to 2min for sim rows specifically. Incident: 2026-04-14 session where Tom was locked out repeatedly.

title: Stricter integer parsing on recording route path/query params
description:
Filed during nucleus-phone-op7 Linus review (P2 nitpick). Current code uses `parseInt(x, 10)` + `isNaN()` which silently accepts `"123abc"` as 123. Theoretical robustness, not a security issue (HMAC verification still gates the stream route).

## What to do

In `server/routes/recording.js` replace:
- `isNaN(callId)` patterns with `Number.isInteger(Number(req.params.callId)) && Number(req.params.callId) > 0`
- Same for `exp` and `u` query params on the stream route

Or extract a small `parsePositiveInt(x)` helper. Add a regression test for `/api/recording/42abc/signed-url` returning 400.

## References

- `server/routes/recording.js:67` (signed-url callId)
- `server/routes/recording.js:120` (stream callId)
- `server/routes/recording.js:128-131` (exp/u parsing)

title: M2 dialer server prerequisites (epic)
description:
## Goal

Server-side prerequisites for the iOS dialer app's M2 milestone (Dial tab: native CallKit + Twilio Voice SDK + conference orchestration). The iOS plan (~/.claude/plans/staged-inventing-key.md, joruva-dialer-mac-upq) treats nucleus-phone as the conference orchestration source of truth — iOS becomes another conference participant. This epic owns the server changes that the iOS plan documents as a contract.

## Why this is a sibling bead, not part of joruva-dialer-mac-upq

Different repos. iOS plan documents the contract; server plan owns implementation. Cross-repo dependency: iOS upq is blocked-on this parent epic.

## Children (filed as separate beads under this epic)

1. live-analysis.js bearer-auth header parse on WebSocket upgrade (in addition to existing cookie path). Server close(1000) on call-end already implemented (verified at lib/live-analysis.js:151-160).
2. lib/twilio.js:generateAccessToken accepts an incomingAllow arg (default false to preserve PWA behavior); routes/token.js accepts ?mode=mobile query param that sets incomingAllow: true. Web continues to call without the param.
3. voice-push.js register route + nucleus_phone_voip_tokens migration + lib/twilio-push-binding.js REST wrapper. POST /api/voice-push/register body { pushToken: hex, environment: 'production'|'sandbox' }.
4. incoming.js iosIdentity routing — extends INBOUND_ROUTES schema, validator, and TwiML branch. Per-route opt-in; legacy <Number> path preserved. Highest-risk server edit.
5. /api/call/active extension at server/routes/call.js:179 — add ?identity=<me> query filter. Response contract iOS depends on: each calls[] element must include startedBy (already populated at lib/conference.js:9) AND type: 'live'.
6. New endpoint GET /api/contacts/lookup?phone=<e164> wrapping lib/hubspot.js:findContactByPhone. Returns { name, company, hubspotId } | null. Field projection: name = properties.firstname + ' ' + properties.lastname, company = properties.company, hubspotId = top-level id. Add a 5s server-side cache by phone.
7. Three-way auth composer + swap on every iOS-called route. Step A: add bearerOrApiKeyOrSession to server/middleware/auth.js (tries bearer → x-api-key → session cookie). Preserve existing authSource string values exactly: 'bearer' (auth.js:157), 'api_key' (auth.js:194 — underscore), 'session'. Step B: swap apiKeyAuth → bearerOrApiKeyOrSession in six files: routes/call.js:21 (callerGuard), routes/cockpit.js:26+71, routes/history.js:234, index.js:73 (token mount + add import at index.js:22), routes/contacts.js (FIVE occurrences: 14, 24, 59, 75, 140), routes/scoreboard.js:17+70. Plus contacts.js:109 includeSummary check: change to allow authSource === 'bearer' alongside 'session'. Net-new endpoints (#3, #6) implemented with bearerOrApiKeyOrSession from the start.

## Blocks

joruva-dialer-mac-upq (M2 iOS dialer epic) — Phase D, E, F, G, H2 all depend on this epic landing.

## Reference

iOS plan: /Users/outpost/.claude/plans/staged-inventing-key.md (lines 350-440 for the full server-bead spec). The plan went through 15 plan-reviewer passes; every server-side detail in this epic was verified against current source.

title: live-analysis.js — bearer-auth header parse on WS upgrade
description: Add Authorization: Bearer <jwt> header parsing alongside the existing nucleus_session cookie path on the WebSocket upgrade handler at server/lib/live-analysis.js:36-57. iOS URLSessionWebSocketTask doesn't send cookies — would 401 on every connect without this. Server-initiated close(1000) on call-end already implemented (verified at live-analysis.js:151-160). Acceptance: bearer-token request hits /api/live-analysis with valid JWT and connects; cookie-based callers continue to work unchanged.

title: lib/twilio.js + routes/token.js — ?mode=mobile flag for incomingAllow:true
description: Parameterize generateAccessToken(identity, { incomingAllow }) in lib/twilio.js (currently hardcoded incomingAllow:false at line 23). Default false to preserve PWA behavior. routes/token.js accepts ?mode=mobile query param that passes incomingAllow:true. Web continues to call without the param. Acceptance: GET /api/token (no param) returns token with incomingAllow:false (PWA unchanged); GET /api/token?mode=mobile returns token with incomingAllow:true (iOS works). Twilio Voice iOS SDK only delivers TVOCallInvite if the registered token had incomingAllow:true — Phase H2 of the iOS plan blocks on this.

title: voice-push.js register route + nucleus_phone_voip_tokens migration
description: POST /api/voice-push/register accepts { pushToken: hex, environment: 'production'|'sandbox' }. Server creates a Twilio Voice push credential binding via REST (lib/twilio-push-binding.js wrapper around Twilio's binding API) using TWILIO_VOICE_PUSH_CREDENTIAL_SID[_SANDBOX] env var based on environment flag. Stores binding SID + push token in new nucleus_phone_voip_tokens table (user_id PK, push_token, voice_credential_binding_sid, environment, updated_at). Auth via bearerOrApiKeyOrSession (depends on child task: three-way auth composer). Validates environment matches the SID being used (sandbox token against production binding silently fails — reject mismatches at registration). Acceptance: iOS POSTs hex token + env flag; server creates Twilio binding + upserts table row; subsequent inbound calls trigger VoIP push to the registered device.

title: incoming.js — iosIdentity routing for inbound TwiML
description:
Extend INBOUND_ROUTES JSON schema to accept iosIdentity?: string. When present, TwiML <Dial><Client>${iosIdentity}</Client></Dial> instead of <Number>${forward}</Number>. Highest-risk server edit — touches the production inbound path for every rep.

Required edits at server/routes/incoming.js:
- Startup validator (line 32-42): change to 'if (routes.length === 0 || !routes.every(([, v]) => v.forward || v.iosIdentity))' — preserves zero-route rejection AND adds new per-route 'forward || iosIdentity' requirement.
- resolveRoute return shape: add iosIdentity?: string to returned object.
- TwiML branch (around line 106): when resolved route has iosIdentity, use <Client>; SKIP the createConference call that uses to: forward (lead-dial-into-conference doesn't apply when no PSTN to dial; iOS-side flow creates the conference). Existing <Number>${forward}</Number> + createConference path unchanged when iosIdentity absent.

Tests (4 cases): (a) legacy route with only forward — uses <Number> + creates PSTN conference (regression test for unchanged path), (b) iOS-only route with only iosIdentity — uses <Client>, no PSTN conference, (c) hybrid route with both fields — iosIdentity wins (documented preference), (d) malformed route with neither — server fails to start (validator catches at boot).

title: /api/call/active — add ?identity=<me> filter + type:'live' field
description:
Extend EXISTING endpoint at server/routes/call.js:179 with ?identity=<me> query filter (server-side narrows the in-memory activeConferences array before sending). The endpoint already exists; no new route. Used by iOS OutboundCallCoordinator as a precondition check before initiating a call (decision: reject iOS dial when agent has active conference on another device).

Response contract iOS depends on: each calls[] element must include startedBy (already populated at lib/conference.js:9) AND type: 'live'. Verify the existing response shape includes type — if it doesn't, add it (likely hardcoded 'live' since the in-memory map represents live conferences exclusively).

iOS filters: calls.some(c => c.startedBy === identity && c.type === 'live').

Acceptance: GET /api/call/active?identity=tom returns { calls: [...] } where every entry has startedBy === 'tom' and type === 'live'; without the param, returns the full unfiltered array (preserves existing behavior). Phase D fail-open policy means iOS treats 5xx as 'no active call' and proceeds to dial.

title: GET /api/contacts/lookup?phone=<e164> — phone-to-contact endpoint
description:
New endpoint wrapping lib/hubspot.js:findContactByPhone (already implemented). Returns { name, company, hubspotId } | null (200 with null for miss; 404 reserved for hard errors).

Field projection: findContactByPhone returns full HubSpot contact object — endpoint must project: name = properties.firstname + ' ' + properties.lastname, company = properties.company, hubspotId = id (top-level).

Add 5s server-side cache by phone number to avoid burning HubSpot rate limit on repeated calls from the same lead (mirrors auth.js:64-76 identity-cache pattern).

Auth: bearerOrApiKeyOrSession from the start (depends on child task: three-way auth composer).

Required by Phase H2 of the iOS plan — incoming-call display flow does a 2s-budgeted lookup to convert E.164 phone to lead name, then live-updates the CallKit display via provider.reportCall(with:updated:). Apple supports live updates so phone shows immediately at ~50ms and name appears within ~500ms on cache hits.

Acceptance: GET /api/contacts/lookup?phone=%2B14802221234 returns { name: 'Tom Russo', company: 'Acme', hubspotId: '12345' } if HubSpot has a match, else { name: null, company: null, hubspotId: null } or 404.

title: Three-way auth composer (bearerOrApiKeyOrSession) + swap on 6 files
description:
Step A — add new middleware bearerOrApiKeyOrSession to server/middleware/auth.js. Tries bearer (if Authorization header present) → API key (if x-api-key present) → session cookie. Preserves all three caller types — web, iOS, automation. CRITICAL: preserve existing req.user.authSource string values exactly: 'bearer' (auth.js:157), 'api_key' (auth.js:194 — underscore not camelCase), 'session'. Downstream code discriminates on these strings (e.g. contacts.js:109 includeSummary, token.js:15 req.user.id !== 0 for synthetic API-key principal — id:0 must be preserved).

Step B — swap apiKeyAuth → bearerOrApiKeyOrSession in 6 files:
- server/routes/call.js:21 — callerGuard constant; affects /api/call/initiate, /active, /end, /mute. Phase D iOS blocks on this.
- server/routes/cockpit.js:26,71 — both routes. Phase E blocks.
- server/routes/history.js:234 — POST /api/history/:id/disposition. Phase G blocks. CRITICAL: existing comment says 'Stays on apiKeyAuth so automation can save dispositions' — three-way composer preserves API-key automation (don't break n8n/scripts).
- server/index.js:73 — /api/token mount-level guard (token.js itself has no auth, relies on the mount). Add bearerOrApiKeyOrSession to imports at index.js:22 (currently only imports apiKeyAuth, sessionAuth). Phase D step 3 blocks.
- server/routes/contacts.js — FIVE occurrences: line 14 (router.use blanket) AND inline at lines 24, 59, 75, 140. Inline guards run AFTER router-level guard — without swapping all five, iOS bearer callers pass router-level then 401 on inline apiKeyAuth.
- server/routes/scoreboard.js:17,70 — not used by iOS in M2 but keep parity for M3+.

Plus: server/routes/contacts.js:109 — change 'const includeSummary = req.user?.authSource === "session";' to allow 'bearer' alongside 'session' (only API-key automation continues to receive stripped response).

Acceptance: iOS bearer JWT calls all five iOS-touched routes successfully; web cookie callers unchanged; n8n/script callers using x-api-key unchanged. Run grep -n apiKeyAuth server/routes/*.js server/index.js BEFORE implementing to confirm no further occurrences appear post-swap.

title: Add m365_create_user tool to joruva-mcp-m365
Conversation Navigator: Backend pipeline — conversation-pipeline.js + wiring
description:
## Context

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/declarative-crunching-wadler.md (Phase 1, Epic 1)
Depends on: nucleus-phone-ns9 (validation script must confirm Haiku latency/quality first)

## What to do

Create the core conversation pipeline module and wire it into the existing transcription routes. This is the Tier 3 (batched, ~8s) analysis layer only. Tier 0/1/2 are in a separate issue.

### 1. Create `server/lib/conversation-pipeline.js`

New module mirroring `server/lib/equipment-pipeline.js` (262 lines). In-memory state per call:

```javascript
const callState = new Map(); // callId -> { buffer, lastAnalysis, history, lastPhase, prediction, phaseBank, sentimentHistory, analysisInFlight, eventLog, consecutiveFailures }
```

**Core flow** (Tier 3 only for this issue):
- `processConversationChunk(callId, text)` accumulates text in buffer
- When buffer >= 3 chunks OR >= 8s since lastAnalysis: send rolling ~60s transcript window to Haiku
- Haiku returns: `{ phase, sentiment, suggestion?, objection?, predicted_next?, phase_bank? }`
- Broadcast phase change via `broadcast()` from `live-analysis.js` (only if phase differs from lastPhase)
- Broadcast `sentiment_update` every cycle (includes sentimentHistory[], capped at 20 entries)
- Broadcast `response_suggestion` when suggestion present
- Broadcast `objection_detected` when objection present
- Reset buffer, update lastAnalysis timestamp
- `analysisInFlight` flag prevents concurrent Haiku calls

**Haiku prompt**: System prompt with prompt caching (cache_control ephemeral). Include:
- PRODUCT_CATALOG from `server/lib/claude.js:18-25` (copy the string, don't import to avoid coupling)
- Common objection/rebuttal pairs
- Instructions per plan: only emit suggestion when customer asks direct question, raises concern, or shows buying interest. 1-2 sentences max.
- Sentiment-aware suggestions: negative/guarded → de-escalation; positive/building → assertive
- Hostile detection: sentiment=hostile OR momentum=tanking for 2+ consecutive cycles → emit exit-assist suggestion

**Fallback**: 5s timeout on Haiku. On timeout, preserve stale state. Track consecutiveFailures. After 3 failures, broadcast `navigator_status: degraded`. On first success after degraded, broadcast `navigator_status: ok`.

**Event log**: Append timestamped entries for phase changes, suggestions, objections, sentiment shifts, exit-assist. Expose via `getCallEventLog(callId)`.

**Stale state sweep**: 10-minute interval, delete entries older than 30 minutes. Follow pattern from `server/lib/stale-sweep.js`.

**Cleanup**: Export `cleanupConversation(callId)` to clear callState entry.

### 2. Wire into transcription.js

At `server/routes/transcription.js:108` (after existing `processEquipmentChunk` call), add:
```javascript
processConversationChunk(callId, transcriptText).catch((err) => {
  console.error('transcription: conversation pipeline error:', err.message);
});
```

### 3. Wire into sim.js

In `server/routes/sim.js:563` (inside `handleTranscriptEvent()`), after `processEquipmentChunk`, add same fire-and-forget call. The wsCallId is `sim-${simId}`.

### 4. Wire cleanup into live-analysis.js

In `cleanupCall()` at `server/lib/live-analysis.js:151-160`, call `cleanupConversation(callId)`.

### 5. Wire event log into sim scoring

In `server/routes/sim.js`, at the 3 locations where `scoreTranscript()` is called (lines ~445, ~490, ~661):
- Before scoring, call `getCallEventLog(wsCallId)` to capture navigator events
- Pass as 4th arg to `scoreTranscript(transcript, difficulty, identity, navigatorEvents)`
- Must happen BEFORE `cleanupCall()` destroys the state

In `server/lib/sim-scorer.js:66`, add `navigatorEvents = null` as 4th parameter. When present, include in the debrief prompt (NOT the scoring prompt). The debrief should mention navigator events ("The Navigator flagged a pricing objection at 2:30...") but they must NOT affect the numeric score.

### 6. Unit tests

Create `server/lib/__tests__/conversation-pipeline.test.js`:
- Buffer accumulation (3 chunks trigger analysis)
- 8-second timer trigger
- Haiku response parsing (valid, malformed, empty)
- Phase deduplication (same phase not re-broadcast)
- Timeout fallback (stale data preserved)
- Cleanup on call end
- sentimentHistory capped at 20
- Event log accumulation

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `conversation-pipeline.js` created with buffer logic, Haiku prompt w/ caching, broadcast
- [ ] Phase detection, sentiment analysis, suggestion generation, hostile-call exit assist working
- [ ] Wired into transcription.js (fire-and-forget, parallel to equipment pipeline)
- [ ] Wired into sim.js handleTranscriptEvent()
- [ ] Cleanup wired into live-analysis.js cleanupCall()
- [ ] scoreTranscript accepts optional navigatorEvents, includes in debrief only
- [ ] Event log captured before cleanup in sim scoring paths
- [ ] Unit tests passing
- [ ] Deploy backend-only — new WS messages flow but frontend ignores them (zero risk)
close_reason: Completed — validation script gates passed (p95<6s, schema 100%); backend pipeline wired into transcription/sim, cleanup + event log integrated, 19 unit tests passing, all 720 server tests green

title: Resolve sizing-rounding contradiction in Compressed Air Fundamentals guide
description:
## Context

Blake Zweigle flagged a real contradiction in the sizing section of Paul's v2 Fundamentals guide on his 2026-04-28 onboarding call (Fireflies `01KQ9A85A7YSYXNP6H0PFVE186` at 18:27).

**The contradiction Blake hit:**

The guide recommends rotating up after the 1.25x multiplier (the safety margin step) on slide N-1.

The very next slide gives a worked example where the equation result is **47.5 CFM**, applies the 1.25x to get **~59 CFM**, then *recommends the smaller JRS10E (43 CFM)* on the basis of "recommend the smaller amount, go for the bigger close later."

A new rep reading top-to-bottom can't tell whether the rule is "round up" or "squeeze in."

**Tom's clarification on the call (the rule we need in the guide):**

Round up by default. The 1.25x then round-up rule is for the average customer — they don't know their CFM precisely, and headroom is the safe play. Only squeeze down (recommend the smaller unit) when the buyer is a true expert who already knows their own demand profile and is asking for your opinion on whether the smaller unit will fit.

The current guide reads as if "squeeze in for the smaller close" is the default playbook. Tom's actual rule is the opposite.

## Where it goes

- Source: `/Users/Shared/nucleus-phone/public/compressed-air-fundamentals.html`
- Find the sizing example slide(s) and either remove the smaller-unit recommendation OR rewrite to make the "true expert exception" explicit.

## Tom committed to this

In the 2026-04-28 email to Blake: "The rounding contradiction in the sizing slides. I'll clean that up. The rule is round up by default. Squeeze down only when the buyer is a true expert who already knows their CFM and is asking your opinion."

## Note

Loop Paul on the change before merging — he wrote v2 of this guide and may have intent we don't see. The guide is currently the canonical reference all reps study before live calls; consistency matters more than diplomatic phrasing.
